def_en: The right or opportunity to use or enter something; to obtain or retrieve
quote_en: Students need a password to access the school's online library.
quote_zh: 学生需要密码才能进入学校的在线图书馆。
ex_en: The greatest wealth is not what you own, but what you can access when you truly need it.
ex_zh: 最大的财富不是你拥有什么，而是当你真正需要时能够获得什么。

def_en: To declare someone not guilty of a crime; to conduct oneself well
quote_en: The jury decided to acquit the defendant after new evidence appeared.
quote_zh: 在新证据出现后，陪审团决定宣判被告无罪。
ex_en: Life will acquit you of your fears only when you stop letting them dictate your choices.
ex_zh: 只有当你不再让恐惧支配你的选择时，生活才会宣告你无罪。

def_en: To change or adjust to new conditions
quote_en: Chameleons can adapt to their surroundings by changing color.
quote_zh: 变色龙可以通过改变颜色来适应周围环境。
ex_en: The strongest people are not those who never break, but those who learn to adapt after every fall.
ex_zh: 最坚强的人不是从未崩溃的人，而是每一次跌倒后都学会适应的人。

def_en: Very skilled or proficient at something
quote_en: She is adept at solving Rubik's cubes in under a minute.
quote_zh: 她擅长在一分钟内解开魔方。
ex_en: Being adept at speaking is useful, but being adept at listening is where true understanding begins.
ex_zh: 擅长说话是有用的，但真正理解始于擅长倾听。

def_en: The management or running of an organization; the people in charge
quote_en: The school administration decided to extend lunch break by ten minutes.
quote_zh: 学校管理部门决定将午休延长十分钟。
ex_en: Good administration keeps a system running; great administration makes everyone feel seen.
ex_zh: 好的管理让系统运转；伟大的管理让每个人都感到被看见。

def_en: To take something as one's own; to legally take a child into one's family
quote_en: The family decided to adopt a rescue dog from the local shelter.
quote_zh: 那家人决定从当地收容所收养一只救助犬。
ex_en: Adopt a mindset of growth, and every mistake becomes a lesson instead of a wound.
ex_zh: 采用成长的心态，每一次错误都会变成教训而非伤痕。

def_en: Excessive praise or flattery
quote_en: The celebrity grew tired of the constant adulation from fans who never really knew him.
quote_zh: 那位名人厌倦了从未真正了解他的粉丝们不断的吹捧。
ex_en: Adulation is a sweet poison; drink it too long, and you forget who you are without it.
ex_zh: 奉承是甜美的毒药；喝得太久，你会忘记没有它时你是谁。

def_en: Ordered as separate items from a menu, not as a set meal
quote_en: At the restaurant, we ordered a la carte instead of choosing the fixed dinner套餐.
quote_zh: 在餐厅里，我们选择了单点而不是固定的套餐。
ex_en: Life does not come a la carte; you often have to accept the whole plate, not just your favorites.
ex_zh: 生活不是单点的菜单；你常常必须接受整个盘子，而不仅仅是你喜欢的部分。

def_en: To distribute or assign resources for a specific purpose
quote_en: The committee will allocate funds to build a new playground.
quote_zh: 委员会将拨出资金建造一个新的游乐场。
ex_en: Allocate your time like you allocate money — spend generously on what matters, and cut what wastes.
ex_zh: 像分配金钱一样分配你的时间——慷慨地花在重要的事情上，削减浪费的部分。

def_en: An indirect reference to something
quote_en: The author made an allusion to Greek mythology in the opening chapter.
quote_zh: 作者在开篇第一章暗指了希腊神话。
ex_en: An allusion can be a bridge between minds — those who understand feel seen, those who don't remain curious.
ex_zh: 暗指可以是心灵之间的桥梁——理解的人感到被看见，不理解的人保持好奇。

def_en: A person who does something for pleasure, not professionally; unskilled
quote_en: The photography contest was open to both amateur and professional photographers.
quote_zh: 摄影比赛对业余和专业摄影师都开放。
ex_en: An amateur practices until he gets it right; a professional practices until he cannot get it wrong.
ex_zh: 业余者练习到做对为止；专业人士练习到不会出错为止。

def_en: To walk at a slow, relaxed pace
quote_en: We decided to amble through the park instead of rushing to the museum.
quote_zh: 我们决定在公园里漫步而不是匆忙赶去博物馆。
ex_en: Sometimes the best discoveries come when you amble instead of race — the journey reveals what the destination hides.
ex_zh: 有时最好的发现来自漫步而非奔跑——旅程揭示了目的地所隐藏的东西。

def_en: A person from whom one is descended, usually from earlier generations
quote_en: My ancestors immigrated to this country over a hundred years ago.
quote_zh: 我的祖先在一百多年前移居到这个国家。
ex_en: An ancestor's greatest gift is not wealth but the lessons carved into their struggles.
ex_zh: 祖先最伟大的礼物不是财富，而是刻在他们奋斗中的教训。

def_en: A collection of literary works, such as poems or stories
quote_en: The teacher assigned an anthology of American short stories for summer reading.
quote_zh: 老师指定了一本美国短篇小说选集作为暑期阅读。
ex_en: Your life is an anthology of moments; choose which chapters to reread and which to close.
ex_zh: 你的人生是时刻的选集；选择重读哪些章节，合上哪些。

def_en: Appealing to the appetite; looking or smelling delicious
quote_en: The smell of freshly baked bread was so appetizing that we bought two loaves.
quote_zh: 新鲜出炉的面包香味如此诱人，我们买了两条。
ex_en: An appetizing invitation is not always good for you; learn to tell hunger from greed.
ex_zh: 诱人的邀请并不总是对你有益；学会区分饥饿与贪婪。

def_en: Anxious or fearful that something bad will happen
quote_en: She felt apprehensive before her first day at a new school.
quote_zh: 她在新学校第一天之前感到忧虑。
ex_en: Being apprehensive about the future is natural, but don't let tomorrow's shadow darken today's light.
ex_zh: 对未来感到忧虑是自然的，但不要让明天的阴影暗淡今天的光明。

def_en: Difficult and tiring; requiring great effort
quote_en: The climb to the mountain peak was arduous, but the view made it worth it.
quote_zh: 攀登到山顶是艰巨的，但景色让一切值得。
ex_en: An arduous path is not a punishment; it is a forge that turns ordinary metal into steel.
ex_zh: 一条艰难的路不是惩罚；它是将普通金属变成钢铁的熔炉。

def_en: A pleasant, distinctive smell
quote_en: The aroma of coffee drifted from the kitchen and filled the whole house.
quote_zh: 咖啡的芳香从厨房飘出，充满了整座房子。
ex_en: A good reputation is like a fine aroma — it travels far, but cannot be faked.
ex_zh: 好的声誉像一缕芳香——它传播很远，但无法伪造。

def_en: An impressive display or range; an ordered arrangement
quote_en: The museum had an impressive array of ancient Egyptian artifacts.
quote_zh: 博物馆陈列着一系列令人印象深刻的古埃及文物。
ex_en: Life offers an array of choices; wisdom is knowing which ones deserve your yes.
ex_zh: 生活提供一系列选择；智慧在于知道哪些值得你说“是”。

def_en: A skilled worker who makes things by hand
quote_en: The artisan spent weeks carving a single wooden chair.
quote_zh: 那位工匠花了数周雕刻一把木椅。
ex_en: An artisan loves the process, not just the product — a lesson for anyone building anything that lasts.
ex_zh: 工匠热爱过程而不仅仅是成品——这对任何建造持久之物的人都是一个教训。

def_en: The act of rising or climbing up
quote_en: The hot air balloon began its slow ascent into the clear blue sky.
quote_zh: 热气球开始缓慢升入清澈的蓝天。
ex_en: Every great ascent begins with a single step upward — and the courage to not look down.
ex_zh: 每一次伟大的攀登都始于向上的一步——以及不往下看的勇气。

def_en: To connect someone or something in one's mind; a work partner
quote_en: People often associate the color red with danger or excitement.
quote_zh: 人们常常将红色与危险或兴奋联系起来。
ex_en: Associate yourself with people who challenge you to grow, not those who make you comfortable in staying small.
ex_zh: 与那些挑战你成长的人为伍，而不是那些让你安于渺小的人。

def_en: To suppose something without proof; to take on a role or duty
quote_en: I assume you have finished your homework since you're watching TV.
quote_zh: 我假设你已经完成了作业，因为你正在看电视。
ex_en: To assume is to bet on an unknown card; ask instead, and you turn the card face up.
ex_zh: 假设是在一张未知的牌上下注；去问吧，你就把牌翻过来了。

def_en: To regard something as caused by someone or something; a quality
quote_en: She attributes her success to hard work and supportive parents.
quote_zh: 她把成功归因于努力工作和支持她的父母。
ex_en: Patience is an attribute that cannot be bought, only built — one small delay at a time.
ex_zh: 耐心是一种无法购买、只能建造的品质——一次一个小小的延迟。

def_en: So common as to be uninteresting; trite
quote_en: The movie's plot was banal — the hero saves the world again with no surprises.
quote_zh: 这部电影的情节很平庸——英雄又一次拯救世界，毫无惊喜。
ex_en: A banal life is not the result of fate, but of choosing comfort over curiosity.
ex_zh: 平庸的人生不是命运的结果，而是选择舒适而非好奇的结果。

def_en: To send someone away; to drive out a thought or feeling
quote_en: The wizard used a spell to banish the dark spirit from the castle.
quote_zh: 巫师用咒语将黑暗精灵从城堡中驱逐出去。
ex_en: Banish the word "impossible" from your vocabulary; what replaces it will reshape your world.
ex_zh: 从你的词汇中驱逐“不可能”这个词；取而代之的将重塑你的世界。

def_en: Extremely cruel or brutal; uncivilized
quote_en: The barbarous treatment of prisoners shocked the international community.
quote_zh: 对囚犯的野蛮待遇震惊了国际社会。
ex_en: A barbarous act is not defined by the weapon, but by the absence of empathy behind it.
ex_zh: 野蛮的行为不是由武器定义的，而是由其背后缺乏共情所定义。

def_en: Unable to produce crops or children; empty of life or interest
quote_en: The desert landscape was barren, with no plants or animals in sight.
quote_zh: 沙漠地貌是贫瘠的，看不到任何植物或动物。
ex_en: A barren mind is not one with few facts, but one that has stopped asking questions.
ex_zh: 贫瘠的心智不是事实稀少的心智，而是停止提问的心智。

def_en: To exchange goods or services without using money
quote_en: The villagers would barter honey for wool at the weekly market.
quote_zh: 村民们在每周集市上用蜂蜜交换羊毛。
ex_en: When you barter your integrity for approval, you always overpay.
ex_zh: 当你用正直换取认可时，你总是支付过多。

def_en: To happen to someone, especially something bad
quote_en: Whatever misfortune may befall you, remember that you are not alone.
quote_zh: 无论什么不幸降临到你身上，记住你不是一个人。
ex_en: We cannot control what befalls us, but we can control what we become afterward.
ex_zh: 我们无法控制降临在我们身上的事，但我们可以控制我们之后成为什么样的人。

def_en: Suffering the death of a loved one
quote_en: The bereaved family received flowers and cards from neighbors.
quote_zh: 失去亲人的家庭收到了邻居送来的鲜花和卡片。
ex_en: A bereaved heart takes time to heal — not by forgetting, but by learning to carry love without the loved one.
ex_zh: 失去亲人的心需要时间愈合——不是通过遗忘，而是通过学会在没有所爱之人的情况下继续承载爱。

def_en: To rouse or stir oneself into action
quote_en: After sleeping in, he finally bestirred himself to clean his room.
quote_zh: 睡过头后，他终于打起精神打扫了房间。
ex_en: The world will not bestir itself for you; you must bestir yourself for the world.
ex_zh: 世界不会为你振奋；你必须为世界振奋自己。

def_en: To give or present something as a gift or honor
quote_en: The queen bestowed a knighthood on the brave soldier.
quote_zh: 女王授予勇敢的士兵爵士头衔。
ex_en: The greatest honor you can bestow on another is your full, undivided attention.
ex_zh: 你能给予他人的最大荣誉，是你全心全意的关注。

def_en: Noisy, energetic, and cheerful
quote_en: The boisterous crowd cheered as the winning goal was scored.
quote_zh: 当制胜球得分时，喧闹的人群欢呼起来。
ex_en: Boisterous laughter is not rudeness; it is the sound of a heart too full to be quiet.
ex_zh: 喧闹的笑声不是粗鲁；那是一颗满得无法安静的心发出的声音。

def_en: Used to express good wishes before a journey
quote_en: Before they boarded the ship, we shouted "Bon voyage!" and waved goodbye.
quote_zh: 在他们登船之前，我们喊道“一路平安！”并挥手告别。
ex_en: Life itself is a voyage; bon voyage to those who sail with courage and return with stories.
ex_zh: 人生本身是一次航行；祝愿那些带着勇气出海、带着故事归来的人一路平安。

def_en: To raise animals or plants; a particular type or variety
quote_en: Farmers breed sheep for wool and meat.
quote_zh: 农民养羊是为了羊毛和肉。
ex_en: Familiarity does not always breed contempt; sometimes it breeds the deepest loyalty.
ex_zh: 熟悉并不总是滋生轻蔑；有时它滋生最深沉的忠诚。

def_en: Rough, coarse, or stupid; like a brute
quote_en: His brutish behavior at the dinner table embarrassed everyone.
quote_zh: 他在餐桌上的粗野行为让所有人都感到尴尬。
ex_en: A brutish response to a gentle word is like punching a flower — you only hurt yourself.
ex_zh: 对温柔话语的野蛮回应就像打一朵花——你只会伤到自己。

def_en: Having the ability or skill to do something
quote_en: You are more capable than you think — you just haven't tried yet.
quote_zh: 你比你想象的更有能力——你只是还没有尝试。
ex_en: Being capable is about skill; being willing is about heart. You need both.
ex_zh: 有能力关乎技能；愿意关乎心。两者都需要。

def_en: The hundredth anniversary of an event
quote_en: The town celebrated the centenary of its founding with a parade and fireworks.
quote_zh: 小镇以游行和烟花庆祝建镇一百周年。
ex_en: A centenary reminds us that what lasts is not built in a day, but in ten thousand small days.
ex_zh: 一百周年纪念提醒我们，持久的东西不是一天建成的，而是在一万个平凡的日子里建成的。

def_en: The person who directs the camera work in a film
quote_en: The cinematographer used soft lighting to create a dreamlike atmosphere.
quote_zh: 电影摄影师使用柔和的灯光营造出梦幻般的氛围。
ex_en: A cinematographer paints with light; you paint your life with the moments you choose to illuminate.
ex_zh: 电影摄影师用光作画；你用你选择照亮的那一刻来描绘你的人生。

def_en: To quote as evidence or an example; to mention formally
quote_en: In her essay, she cited three scientists to support her argument.
quote_zh: 在她的论文中，她引用了三位科学家来支持她的论点。
ex_en: To cite a fact is easy; to live by it is the real test of belief.
ex_zh: 引用一个事实很容易；按照它生活才是信念的真正考验。

def_en: Loud, confused noise; to make a noisy demand
quote_en: The clamor of the students in the cafeteria could be heard down the hall.
quote_zh: 食堂里学生们的喧闹声走廊尽头都能听到。
ex_en: Do not mistake clamor for truth; the loudest voice is rarely the wisest.
ex_zh: 不要把喧闹误认为真理；最响亮的声音很少是最明智的。

def_en: A person who pays for professional services
quote_en: The lawyer met with her client to discuss the case.
quote_zh: 律师与她的客户会面讨论案件。
ex_en: Treat every client as if they were your only one — that is the secret of trust.
ex_zh: 把每一位客户当作你唯一的一位来对待——这是信任的秘诀。

def_en: To persuade gently or gradually
quote_en: She coaxed the shy kitten out from under the bed with a toy.
quote_zh: 她用玩具把害羞的小猫从床底下哄了出来。
ex_en: You cannot coax a tree to grow faster; you can only water it and wait. People are the same.
ex_zh: 你无法哄一棵树长得更快；你只能浇水等待。人也是一样。

def_en: To exist together at the same time or place
quote_en: Different religions and cultures can coexist peacefully in the same city.
quote_zh: 不同的宗教和文化可以在同一座城市和平共存。
ex_en: To coexist is not to agree on everything, but to disagree without destroying each other.
ex_zh: 共存不是事事赞同，而是在不毁灭彼此的前提下持有不同意见。

def_en: To work jointly on an activity or project
quote_en: The two companies collaborated to develop a new electric car.
quote_zh: 两家公司合作开发了一款新的电动汽车。
ex_en: When you collaborate, one plus one can equal three — if you bring your best and trust the other.
ex_zh: 当你合作时，一加一可以等于三——如果你拿出最好的并信任对方。

def_en: To collect and arrange information or pages in order
quote_en: Please collate the handouts by putting the pages in numerical order.
quote_zh: 请按页码顺序整理讲义。
ex_en: To collate facts is to gather bricks; to build wisdom is to lay them in the right order.
ex_zh: 整理事实是收集砖块；建立智慧是按照正确的顺序铺设它们。

def_en: To honor the memory of someone or something
quote_en: A statue was built to commemorate the firefighters who saved the town.
quote_zh: 建造了一座雕像来纪念拯救小镇的消防员。
ex_en: We commemorate the past not to live in it, but to thank it for the lessons it taught us.
ex_zh: 我们纪念过去不是为了活在其中，而是为了感谢它教给我们的教训。

def_en: The activity of buying and selling goods and services
quote_en: Online commerce has grown rapidly with websites like Amazon and eBay.
quote_zh: 随着亚马逊和eBay等网站的出现，在线商业迅速发展。
ex_en: Commerce without conscience is exploitation; trade with integrity builds lasting bridges.
ex_zh: 没有良知的商业是剥削；有诚信的交易建造持久的桥梁。

def_en: To mix or blend together
quote_en: The artist allowed different colors to commingle on the canvas.
quote_zh: 艺术家让不同的颜色在画布上混合。
ex_en: When joy and sorrow commingle, you get the deep flavor of a life fully lived.
ex_zh: 当喜悦与悲伤混合时，你得到了充分活过一生的深沉滋味。

def_en: Ordinary, not unusual
quote_en: Smartphones are commonplace now, but twenty years ago they were a luxury.
quote_zh: 智能手机现在很常见，但二十年前它们还是奢侈品。
ex_en: A commonplace act of kindness — holding a door, saying thank you — can be extraordinary to someone who is hurting.
ex_zh: 一个平凡的善举——扶一下门，说一声谢谢——对一个正在受伤的人来说可能是非凡的。

def_en: Something given to make up for loss, injury, or work
quote_en: The worker received financial compensation for his injury on the job.
quote_zh: 工人因工伤获得了经济补偿。
ex_en: The only compensation for a misspent day is a well‑spent tomorrow.
ex_zh: 对虚度一天的唯一补偿是一个充实的明天。

def_en: Something that completes or makes perfect; to go well with
quote_en: A good wine can complement a meal, but it cannot replace good company.
quote_zh: 好酒可以为餐食增色，但不能取代好伙伴。
ex_en: Your strengths and weaknesses are not enemies; they complement each other like two sides of a coin.
ex_zh: 你的优点和缺点不是敌人；它们像硬币的两面一样相互补充。

def_en: A polite expression of praise or admiration
quote_en: She blushed when he gave her a compliment about her singing voice.
quote_zh: 当他赞美她的歌声时，她脸红了。
ex_en: A sincere compliment costs nothing but can buy someone a day's worth of hope.
ex_zh: 一句真诚的赞美不花分文，却能为某人买来一整天的希望。

def_en: An agreement reached by each side giving up something
quote_en: The siblings reached a compromise: one chose the movie, the other chose the snacks.
quote_zh: 兄妹俩达成了妥协：一个选电影，另一个选零食。
ex_en: Compromise is not weakness; it is the wisdom of keeping a relationship alive when being right would kill it.
ex_zh: 妥协不是软弱；它是在正确会杀死一段关系时让关系存活的智慧。

def_en: To admit that something is true or to give up a contest
quote_en: After hours of debate, he finally conceded that his opponent had a point.
quote_zh: 经过数小时的辩论，他终于承认对手说得有道理。
ex_en: To concede a small point is not to lose an argument; it is to win respect.
ex_zh: 承认一个小论点不是输掉争论；而是赢得尊重。

def_en: A private or secret meeting
quote_en: The cardinals gathered in a conclave to elect a new pope.
quote_zh: 红衣主教们聚集在秘密会议上选举新教皇。
ex_en: A conclave can be a place of wisdom or of plotting — the difference lies in the hearts of those inside.
ex_zh: 秘密会议可以是智慧的场所，也可以是密谋的场所——区别在于内部之人的心。

def_en: To agree or have the same opinion
quote_en: I concur with your assessment that the project needs more time.
quote_zh: 我同意你的评估，这个项目需要更多时间。
ex_en: You can concur with someone without abandoning your own voice; agreement is not surrender.
ex_zh: 你可以同意某人而不放弃自己的声音；同意不是投降。

def_en: An expression of sympathy, especially after a death
quote_en: We sent a card of condolence to our neighbor whose father passed away.
quote_zh: 我们给父亲去世的邻居寄了一张慰问卡。
ex_en: The deepest condolence is not found in perfect words, but in silent presence.
ex_zh: 最深切的哀悼不在完美的言语中，而在沉默的陪伴中。

def_en: To grant or bestow; to discuss or consult
quote_en: The university will confer honorary degrees on three distinguished scientists.
quote_zh: 大学将授予三位杰出科学家荣誉学位。
ex_en: Before you confer blame, confer understanding — you might find there is no blame to give.
ex_zh: 在给予责备之前，先给予理解——你可能会发现没有什么可责备的。

def_en: To tell someone a secret or private matter
quote_en: She decided to confide in her best friend about her worries.
quote_zh: 她决定向最好的朋友吐露她的担忧。
ex_en: To confide is to hand someone a key to your heart; choose the keeper wisely.
ex_zh: 吐露秘密是把一把钥匙交到别人手中；明智地选择保管人。

def_en: To take possession of something by authority
quote_en: The teacher confiscated the student's phone after he used it in class.
quote_zh: 老师在学生课上使用手机后没收了它。
ex_en: You cannot confiscate a person's dignity unless they first give you permission.
ex_zh: 你无法没收一个人的尊严，除非他们先允许你这样做。

def_en: Behavior that follows accepted rules or standards
quote_en: The school's strict dress code encouraged conformity among students.
quote_zh: 学校严格的着装要求鼓励学生遵从统一标准。
ex_en: Conformity keeps the boat steady; nonconformity discovers new shores. Sail wisely.
ex_zh: 遵从让船平稳；不从众发现新海岸。明智地航行。

def_en: A result or effect of an action or condition
quote_en: If you don't water the plants, the consequence is that they will wilt.
quote_zh: 如果你不给植物浇水，后果就是它们会枯萎。
ex_en: Every choice plants a seed; the consequence is the fruit you will harvest later.
ex_zh: 每一个选择都种下一颗种子；后果是你以后将收获的果实。

def_en: A school for music or arts; a glass room for plants
quote_en: She practiced piano for hours at the city's conservatory of music.
quote_zh: 她在城市的音乐学院练了几个小时的钢琴。
ex_en: A conservatory nurtures talent; your home can be a conservatory for kindness.
ex_zh: 音乐学院培育才华；你的家可以是善意的温室。

def_en: The quality of always behaving the same way
quote_en: The team's consistency in practice led to their championship win.
quote_zh: 团队训练的一致性带来了他们的冠军胜利。
ex_en: Consistency is not about being perfect every day; it is about showing up even on the hard days.
ex_zh: 一致性不是每天都完美；而是在艰难的日子里仍然出现。

def_en: Comfort received after a loss or disappointment
quote_en: The runner took consolation in knowing she had tried her best.
quote_zh: 那位赛跑选手因知道自己尽了全力而感到安慰。
ex_en: Consolation is not a prize, but a reminder that loss does not have the last word.
ex_zh: 安慰不是奖品，而是一个提醒：失去不是最后的定论。

def_en: To secretly plan with others to do something unlawful or harmful
quote_en: The thieves conspired to rob the bank under cover of night.
quote_zh: 小偷们密谋在夜幕掩护下抢劫银行。
ex_en: When hope and courage conspire, even impossible mountains begin to move.
ex_zh: 当希望与勇气合谋时，即使不可能的山峰也开始移动。

def_en: An expert who gives professional advice
quote_en: The company hired a consultant to improve their marketing strategy.
quote_zh: 公司聘请了一位顾问来改进他们的营销策略。
ex_en: A good consultant listens more than she speaks; the best answers often live inside you.
ex_zh: 好的顾问倾听多于说话；最好的答案常常就在你心里。

def_en: To think deeply about something
quote_en: He sat by the river to contemplate his future plans.
quote_zh: 他坐在河边思考他的未来计划。
ex_en: To contemplate is not to worry; it is to look at a problem from all sides before choosing a path.
ex_zh: 沉思不是担忧；而是在选择一条路之前从各个角度审视一个问题。

def_en: To come together for a meeting or purpose
quote_en: The student council will convene in the library after school.
quote_zh: 学生会将在放学后在图书馆集合。
ex_en: When minds convene with open hearts, the solutions they find are greater than any one could imagine alone.
ex_zh: 当思想带着开放的心聚集时，它们找到的解决方案比任何一个人独自想象的都要伟大。

def_en: The quality of being easy, suitable, or handy
quote_en: Online shopping offers the convenience of home delivery.
quote_zh: 网上购物提供了送货上门的便利。
ex_en: Convenience can become a trap when you trade what matters for what is easy.
ex_zh: 当你用重要的东西换取容易的东西时，便利就可能成为一个陷阱。

def_en: To change from one form, use, or religion to another
quote_en: The old factory was converted into a modern art museum.
quote_zh: 旧工厂被改造成了一座现代艺术博物馆。
ex_en: The hardest thing to convert is not a building or a belief — it is a closed mind.
ex_zh: 最难改变的不是建筑或信仰——而是封闭的思想。

def_en: To communicate or express; to transport
quote_en: Her smile conveyed more gratitude than any words could.
quote_zh: 她的微笑传达出的感激之情胜过任何言语。
ex_en: The way you convey a truth matters as much as the truth itself; kindness is the best vehicle.
ex_zh: 你传达真相的方式与真相本身同样重要；善意是最好的载体。

def_en: Fat; having a large, bulky body
quote_en: The corpulent cat could barely squeeze through the cat flap.
quote_zh: 那只肥胖的猫几乎挤不过猫洞。
ex_en: A corpulent ego weighs more than any body; shed it, and you will move freely.
ex_zh: 膨胀的自我比任何身体都重；摆脱它，你就能自由移动。

def_en: To crouch down in fear
quote_en: The dog cowered under the table during the thunderstorm.
quote_zh: 雷雨期间，狗蜷缩在桌子底下。
ex_en: Do not cower before a challenge; stand even if you shake — courage is not the absence of fear.
ex_zh: 不要在挑战面前畏缩；即使颤抖也要站立——勇气不是没有恐惧。

def_en: The subjects taught in a school or course
quote_en: The science curriculum includes biology, chemistry, and physics.
quote_zh: 科学课程包括生物学、化学和物理学。
ex_en: The most important part of your curriculum is what you learn when no one is grading you.
ex_zh: 你课程中最重要的部分，是当没有人给你打分时你学到的东西。

def_en: An athletic contest with ten different events
quote_en: The decathlon tests athletes in running, jumping, throwing, and more.
quote_zh: 十项全能测试运动员的跑步、跳跃、投掷等项目。
ex_en: Life is a decathlon; don't judge yourself by one event when there are nine more to go.
ex_zh: 人生是一场十项全能；当还有九项比赛时，不要用一项来评判自己。

def_en: Dead; a person who has died
quote_en: The deceased was remembered by family and friends at a small service.
quote_zh: 家人在一个小型仪式上缅怀了逝者。
ex_en: Honor the deceased not by building statues, but by living the values they cherished.
ex_zh: 纪念逝者不是通过建造雕像，而是通过活出他们所珍视的价值观。

def_en: A person or thing used to lure someone into a trap
quote_en: The hunters used a wooden duck as a decoy to attract real ducks.
quote_zh: 猎人用一只木鸭作为诱饵来吸引真鸭。
ex_en: Do not be a decoy for someone else's ambitions; fly your own path.
ex_zh: 不要成为别人野心的诱饵；飞你自己的路。

def_en: To spoil the appearance of something
quote_en: Vandals defaced the statue with spray paint.
quote_zh: 破坏者用喷漆污损了雕像。
ex_en: To deface another's reputation is to reveal more about your own character than theirs.
ex_zh: 诋毁他人的声誉，更多揭示的是你自己的品格而非他们的。

def_en: A fault or flaw; to abandon one's country or cause
quote_en: The car was recalled due to a defect in the brake system.
quote_zh: 汽车因刹车系统缺陷被召回。
ex_en: A single defect does not make a person worthless; a single virtue does not make them perfect.
ex_zh: 一个缺陷不会让一个人毫无价值；一个美德也不会让他们完美。

def_en: Having a flaw; imperfect
quote_en: The defective toy was returned to the store for a refund.
quote_zh: 有缺陷的玩具被退回商店退款。
ex_en: A defective compass is worse than no compass at all; check your beliefs before you trust them.
ex_zh: 有缺陷的指南针比没有指南针更糟糕；在你相信你的信念之前先检查它们。

def_en: To postpone; to yield to another's opinion
quote_en: We decided to defer our vacation until after the exam period.
quote_zh: 我们决定把假期推迟到考试期之后。
ex_en: To defer a decision is sometimes wise; to defer a kindness is never wise.
ex_zh: 推迟一个决定有时是明智的；推迟一份善意则永远不明智。

def_en: Lacking something essential
quote_en: The soil was deficient in nitrogen, so the plants grew poorly.
quote_zh: 土壤缺乏氮，所以植物长得不好。
ex_en: A life deficient in gratitude is like a sky without stars — functional, but empty.
ex_zh: 缺乏感恩的生活就像没有星星的天空——能运转，但空洞。

def_en: A physical malformation or distortion
quote_en: The injury left a slight deformity in his left hand.
quote_zh: 那次受伤使他的左手留下了轻微的畸形。
ex_en: A deformity of the body is visible; a deformity of the heart hides behind a smile.
ex_zh: 身体的畸形是可见的；心灵的畸形隐藏在微笑背后。

def_en: To treat with disrespect; to break down
quote_en: It is wrong to degrade someone because of their background or appearance.
quote_zh: 因为别人的背景或外表而贬低他们是错误的。
ex_en: To degrade another is to degrade yourself; lifting others lifts everyone.
ex_zh: 贬低他人就是贬低自己；提升他人则提升所有人。

def_en: To lose or remove water
quote_en: If you exercise without drinking water, you will dehydrate quickly.
quote_zh: 如果你不喝水就运动，你会很快脱水。
ex_en: A heart without forgiveness will dehydrate into bitterness; water it with grace.
ex_zh: 没有宽恕的心会脱水成苦毒；用恩典浇灌它。

def_en: Delicious; highly pleasing
quote_en: The chocolate cake was so delectable that everyone asked for seconds.
quote_zh: 巧克力蛋糕如此美味，每个人都要求再来一份。
ex_en: A delectable meal feeds the body; a delectable word feeds the spirit.
ex_zh: 美味的餐食喂养身体；愉悦的话语喂养精神。

def_en: To entrust a task to another; a representative
quote_en: The manager decided to delegate some tasks to her assistant.
quote_zh: 经理决定把一些任务委派给她的助理。
ex_en: To delegate is not to escape responsibility; it is to trust others and multiply your impact.
ex_zh: 委派不是逃避责任；而是信任他人并倍增你的影响力。

def_en: Intentional; done on purpose; to think carefully
quote_en: His insult was deliberate, not an accident.
quote_zh: 他的侮辱是故意的，不是意外。
ex_en: A deliberate life is not a perfect life, but one where every choice has been thought through.
ex_zh: 深思熟虑的人生不是完美的人生，而是每一个选择都经过深思熟虑的人生。

def_en: Easily broken or damaged; requiring careful handling
quote_en: The delicate glass ornament must be wrapped in bubble wrap.
quote_zh: 精致的玻璃装饰品必须用气泡膜包裹。
ex_en: A delicate situation calls for a gentle tongue; harsh words can shatter what took years to build.
ex_zh: 微妙的情况需要温和的舌头；严厉的言语可能粉碎多年建立的东西。

def_en: A store selling cold cuts, cheeses, and prepared foods
quote_en: We bought fresh sandwiches and pickles from the local delicatessen.
quote_zh: 我们从当地的熟食店买了新鲜的三明治和泡菜。
ex_en: A delicatessen offers a feast of choices; life offers the same — taste wisely.
ex_zh: 熟食店提供盛宴般的选择；生活也是如此——明智地品尝。

def_en: To publicly declare something to be wrong or evil
quote_en: The mayor denounced the vandalism and promised to find those responsible.
quote_zh: 市长谴责了破坏行为并承诺找到责任人。
ex_en: To denounce an injustice without offering a better way is to throw stones without building shelter.
ex_zh: 谴责不公而不提供更好的方法，就像扔石头而不建造庇护所。

def_en: The state of relying on someone or something
quote_en: His dependency on his phone for directions made him lost when the battery died.
quote_zh: 他依赖手机导航，电池耗尽后就迷路了。
ex_en: Healthy dependency is trust; unhealthy dependency is a cage. Know the difference.
ex_zh: 健康的依赖是信任；不健康的依赖是牢笼。知道区别。

def_en: To use up or reduce drastically
quote_en: Years of overfishing have depleted the ocean's fish stocks.
quote_zh: 多年的过度捕捞已经耗尽了海洋的鱼类资源。
ex_en: Worry depletes your energy faster than any physical labor; save your strength for action.
ex_zh: 担忧比任何体力劳动更快耗尽你的精力；把你的力量留给行动。

def_en: To deny someone of something needed
quote_en: The prisoners were deprived of food and water for two days.
quote_zh: 囚犯们被剥夺食物和水两天。
ex_en: To deprive yourself of rest is to steal from tomorrow's energy. Sleep is not weakness.
ex_zh: 剥夺自己的休息就是从明天的精力中偷窃。睡眠不是软弱。

def_en: To move downward; to come from an ancestor
quote_en: The elevator began to descend slowly to the ground floor.
quote_zh: 电梯开始缓慢下降到一楼。
ex_en: When darkness descends, remember that dawn is not far behind — you just cannot see it yet.
ex_zh: 当黑暗降临时，记住黎明就在不远处——你只是还看不到它。

def_en: To keep someone from leaving; to delay
quote_en: The police detained the suspect for questioning.
quote_zh: 警察扣留嫌疑人进行讯问。
ex_en: Do not detain a friend with a promise you cannot keep; free them with honesty.
ex_zh: 不要用一个你无法遵守的承诺扣留朋友；用诚实释放他们。

def_en: The act of being kept in custody; a punishment in school
quote_en: He got detention for arriving late to class three times.
quote_zh: 他因三次上课迟到被留校。
ex_en: Self-imposed detention in the prison of regret helps no one; release yourself and move forward.
ex_zh: 在悔恨的监狱里自我拘留对谁都没有帮助；释放自己，向前走。

def_en: To become progressively worse
quote_en: The old building's condition deteriorated after years of neglect.
quote_zh: 多年无人照管后，那座旧建筑的状态恶化了。
ex_en: A relationship will deteriorate without daily acts of kindness, like a garden without water.
ex_zh: 没有日常的善意行为，一段关系会恶化，就像没有水的花园。

def_en: Something that discourages or prevents an action
quote_en: The high cost of parking is a deterrent to driving downtown.
quote_zh: 高昂的停车费是开车去市区的威慑因素。
ex_en: The best deterrent to a bad habit is not punishment, but a better alternative.
ex_zh: 对坏习惯最好的威慑不是惩罚，而是更好的替代选择。

def_en: Completely lacking
quote_en: The desert is devoid of water for most of the year.
quote_zh: 沙漠一年中大部分时间缺乏水。
ex_en: A life devoid of gratitude is like a song without a melody — it exists, but it does not move.
ex_zh: 缺乏感恩的生活就像没有旋律的歌——它存在，但不打动人。

def_en: Conversation between two or more people
quote_en: The characters in the novel have realistic dialogue that reveals their personalities.
quote_zh: 小说中的人物有揭示他们个性的真实对话。
ex_en: Dialogue is not two monologues colliding; it is listening to understand, not to reply.
ex_zh: 对话不是两个独白的碰撞；它是为了理解而倾听，而不是为了回应。

def_en: To take away weapons; to win over or reduce hostility
quote_en: The guard asked the visitors to disarm before entering the embassy.
quote_zh: 警卫要求来访者在进入大使馆前解除武装。
ex_en: A genuine apology can disarm the angriest heart faster than any argument.
ex_zh: 真诚的道歉能比任何争论更快地消除最愤怒的心。

def_en: A state of disorder or confusion
quote_en: Her desk was in complete disarray, covered with papers and books.
quote_zh: 她的书桌一片混乱，堆满了纸张和书籍。
ex_en: When your mind is in disarray, start with one small action — order begins with a single step.
ex_zh: 当你的思想混乱时，从一个微小的行动开始——秩序始于一步。

def_en: To deny any responsibility or support for
quote_en: The politician tried to disavow the statements made by his former advisor.
quote_zh: 那位政治家试图否认他前顾问发表的言论。
ex_en: To disavow a mistake is to miss the lesson; to own it is to grow.
ex_zh: 否认错误就是错过教训；承认它才能成长。

def_en: To refuse to acknowledge or accept
quote_en: The company disclaims any liability for damage caused by misuse.
quote_zh: 公司否认对误用造成的损坏承担任何责任。
ex_en: It is easy to disclaim responsibility when things go wrong; it takes courage to say "I was wrong."
ex_zh: 事情出错时推卸责任很容易；说“我错了”需要勇气。

def_en: To make known; to reveal
quote_en: The report did not disclose the names of the victims.
quote_zh: 报告没有披露受害者的名字。
ex_en: To disclose a truth that hurts is better than to conceal a truth that heals. Choose wisely.
ex_zh: 揭露伤人的真相好过隐瞒治愈的真相。明智地选择。

def_en: To harm the reputation of; loss of respect
quote_en: The scandal discredited the once-respected organization.
quote_zh: 丑闻使那个曾经受人尊敬的组织信誉扫地。
ex_en: Do not discredit a whole person because of one flaw; you too are a mosaic of light and shadow.
ex_zh: 不要因为一个缺点就诋毁整个人；你也是光明与阴影的马赛克。

def_en: To release or separate from engagement
quote_en: The pilot had to disengage the autopilot and land manually.
quote_zh: 飞行员不得不解除自动驾驶并手动着陆。
ex_en: Sometimes the wisest action is to disengage from a losing argument — not all battles need a victor.
ex_zh: 有时最明智的行动是从一场必输的争论中脱离——并非所有战斗都需要胜利者。

def_en: Untidy; messy in appearance
quote_en: After the long flight, he looked tired and disheveled.
quote_zh: 长途飞行后，他看起来疲惫而凌乱。
ex_en: A disheveled appearance does not reflect a disordered soul; do not judge by covers.
ex_zh: 凌乱的外表并不反映混乱的灵魂；不要以封面判断。

def_en: To disappoint by revealing the truth about something
quote_en: Working in politics disillusioned her about the system she once admired.
quote_zh: 在政界工作使她对她曾经崇拜的体制感到幻灭。
ex_en: Disillusion is painful, but it is also the first step toward seeing the world as it is — and then changing it.
ex_zh: 幻灭是痛苦的，但它也是看清世界本来面目——然后改变它的第一步。

def_en: A person involved in a dispute or argument
quote_en: The mediator listened to both disputants before offering a solution.
quote_zh: 调解人在提出解决方案之前听取了双方争论者的意见。
ex_en: A disputant who only wants to win learns nothing; one who wants to understand grows wise.
ex_zh: 只想赢的争论者什么也学不到；想要理解的争论者变得智慧。

def_en: Characteristic of one person or thing; distinguishing
quote_en: Her voice is distinctive — you can recognize it immediately.
quote_zh: 她的声音很独特——你能立刻认出来。
ex_en: Your distinctive gift is not a weapon to dominate others, but a light to illuminate their path.
ex_zh: 你独特的天赋不是支配他人的武器，而是照亮他们道路的光。

def_en: To twist or misrepresent
quote_en: The funhouse mirrors distort your reflection, making you look taller or shorter.
quote_zh: 游乐厅的镜子扭曲了你的倒影，让你看起来更高或更矮。
ex_en: Fear distorts reality more than any carnival mirror; pause and check the facts.
ex_zh: 恐惧比任何狂欢节镜子更能扭曲现实；停下来检查事实。

def_en: An alternative route; a distraction
quote_en: Road construction forced a diversion through the side streets.
quote_zh: 道路施工迫使车辆绕行小路。
ex_en: A diversion can be a welcome break, but a constant diversion from your goals is a detour without end.
ex_zh: 转移注意力可以是受欢迎的休息，但不断从你的目标上转移是没有尽头的绕路。

def_en: The state of having many different forms or types
quote_en: The diversity of languages spoken in our school is amazing.
quote_zh: 我们学校所讲语言的多样性令人惊叹。
ex_en: Diversity is not a problem to solve; it is a garden to cultivate — every flower brings a different beauty.
ex_zh: 多样性不是需要解决的问题；它是需要培育的花园——每一朵花带来不同的美。

def_en: A feeling of uncertainty; to question
quote_en: I have no doubt that you will succeed if you keep trying.
quote_zh: 如果你继续努力，我毫不怀疑你会成功。
ex_en: Doubt is not the enemy of faith; certainty without examination is.
ex_zh: 怀疑不是信仰的敌人；不经审视的确信才是。

def_en: A preliminary version; to select for a purpose; to pull air
quote_en: The writer revised her first draft three times before publishing.
quote_zh: 作者在出版前将初稿修改了三次。
ex_en: Life is a rough draft that you edit as you go — there is no final version, only the next version.
ex_zh: 人生是一边进行一边编辑的草稿——没有最终版本，只有下一个版本。

def_en: Hesitating or doubting; of questionable value
quote_en: I am dubious about his claim that he finished the marathon in two hours.
quote_zh: 我对他声称两小时跑完马拉松的说法表示怀疑。
ex_en: A dubious promise is worse than a clear refusal; uncertainty is a shaky foundation.
ex_zh: 可疑的承诺比明确的拒绝更糟糕；不确定性是摇摇欲坠的基础。

def_en: Successful in producing a desired result
quote_en: The new medicine was effectual in reducing symptoms within days.
quote_zh: 新药在几天内有效减轻了症状。
ex_en: An effectual apology requires changed behavior, not just changed words.
ex_zh: 有效的道歉需要改变行为，而不仅仅是改变言语。

def_en: Detailed and complicated; to explain further
quote_en: The wedding cake had an elaborate design with sugar flowers.
quote_zh: 婚礼蛋糕有精美的糖花设计。
ex_en: An elaborate plan is useless if it ignores the simplest truth: people need kindness.
ex_zh: 如果忽略了最简单的真理——人需要善意，那么复杂的计划就是无用的。

def_en: A government order prohibiting trade with another country
quote_en: The United Nations placed an embargo on arms shipments to the warring nation.
quote_zh: 联合国对交战国的武器运输实施了禁运。
ex_en: An embargo on kind words hurts more than any trade ban; speak freely.
ex_zh: 对善意话语的禁运比任何贸易禁令都更伤人；自由地说话。

def_en: To hug; to accept willingly
quote_en: The teacher embraced the new teaching method with enthusiasm.
quote_zh: 老师热情地接受了新的教学方法。
ex_en: Embrace your imperfections — they are not flaws, but the fingerprints of your unique journey.
ex_zh: 拥抱你的不完美——它们不是缺陷，而是你独特旅程的指纹。

def_en: To form a circle around
quote_en: The high mountains encircle the valley, protecting it from harsh winds.
quote_zh: 高山环绕着山谷，保护它免受强风的侵袭。
ex_en: Let gratitude encircle your heart like a ring of light, and darkness will have no room to enter.
ex_zh: 让感恩像光环一样环绕你的心，黑暗就没有空间进入。

def_en: To declare support for; to sign a check
quote_en: The famous athlete endorsed a new brand of running shoes.
quote_zh: 那位著名运动员支持一个新品牌的跑鞋。
ex_en: To endorse someone's effort is to hand them a mirror that reflects their worth — a powerful gift.
ex_zh: 支持某人的努力就是把一面反映他们价值的镜子递给他们——一份强大的礼物。

def_en: A business or project; initiative
quote_en: Starting a small enterprise requires planning, courage, and hard work.
quote_zh: 创办一个小型企业需要规划、勇气和辛勤工作。
ex_en: The greatest enterprise you will ever lead is the building of your own character.
ex_zh: 你将领导的最伟大的事业是你自己品格的建造。

def_en: To place in a tomb; to bury
quote_en: The ancient king was entombed with his treasures and servants.
quote_zh: 古代国王与他的财宝和仆人一同被埋葬。
ex_en: Do not entomb your dreams in the cemetery of "someday"; dig them out and start today.
ex_zh: 不要把梦想埋葬在“某一天”的墓地里；把它们挖出来，从今天开始。

def_en: A long poem or story about heroic deeds; grand in scale
quote_en: The Odyssey is an epic about a hero's long journey home.
quote_zh: 《奥德赛》是一部关于英雄漫长归家之旅的史诗。
ex_en: Your life does not need to be an epic to matter; a quiet act of kindness echoes just as far.
ex_zh: 你的人生不需要成为史诗才有意义；一个安静的善举同样回响深远。

def_en: A short, witty saying
quote_en: "The only way to have a friend is to be one" is an epigram by Emerson.
quote_zh: “拥有朋友的唯一方法是成为朋友”是爱默生的警句。
ex_en: A good epigram fits in a breath but lasts a lifetime — like a seed that grows into a forest.
ex_zh: 一个好的警句一次呼吸就能容纳，却能持续一生——就像长成森林的种子。

def_en: An imaginary line dividing the Earth into northern and southern hemispheres
quote_en: The equator passes through countries like Ecuador, Brazil, and Kenya.
quote_zh: 赤道穿过厄瓜多尔、巴西和肯尼亚等国家。
ex_en: The equator of your soul is the balance point between giving and receiving — stay there.
ex_zh: 你灵魂的赤道是给予与接受之间的平衡点——留在那里。

def_en: A long period of time marked by particular events or characteristics
quote_en: The invention of the internet began a new era of global communication.
quote_zh: 互联网的发明开启了全球交流的新时代。
ex_en: Every ending of an era is also the beginning of another — grieve the old, then turn toward the new.
ex_zh: 一个时代的结束也是另一个时代的开始——哀悼旧的，然后转向新的。

def_en: To roughly calculate or judge
quote_en: The contractor estimated that the repairs would cost about $2,000.
quote_zh: 承包商估计维修费用约为2000美元。
ex_en: You cannot estimate the value of a kind word; it multiplies in ways no calculator can track.
ex_zh: 你无法估计一句善意话语的价值；它以任何计算器都无法追踪的方式倍增。

def_en: To go beyond a limit or expectation
quote_en: The speed limit is 55 miles per hour; do not exceed it.
quote_zh: 限速是每小时55英里；不要超过。
ex_en: Do not just meet expectations — exceed them, not for applause, but because you can.
ex_zh: 不要只是满足期望——超越它们，不是为了掌声，而是因为你能做到。

def_en: Too much; more than is necessary
quote_en: The team spent an excessive amount of time planning and not enough doing.
quote_zh: 团队花了过多时间规划而没有足够时间执行。
ex_en: Excessive worry is like a rocking chair — it gives you something to do but gets you nowhere.
ex_zh: 过度的担忧就像摇椅——它让你有事可做，但哪儿也去不了。

def_en: Serving as a perfect example; outstanding
quote_en: Her exemplary behavior earned her the citizenship award.
quote_zh: 她典范的行为为她赢得了公民奖。
ex_en: An exemplary life is not one without mistakes, but one that uses mistakes as stepping stones.
ex_zh: 典范的人生不是没有错误的人生，而是将错误作为垫脚石的人生。

def_en: To dig up a buried body
quote_en: The police obtained a court order to exhume the body for further examination.
quote_zh: 警方获得法庭命令掘出尸体进行进一步检查。
ex_en: To exhume old grievances is to bury your peace; let the dead past stay buried.
ex_zh: 挖掘旧怨就是埋葬你的平静；让死去的过去保持埋葬。

def_en: A journey for a specific purpose
quote_en: The team launched an expedition to climb Mount Everest.
quote_zh: 团队发起了一次攀登珠穆朗玛峰的远征。
ex_en: Every day is an expedition into the unknown — pack curiosity, not fear.
ex_zh: 每一天都是一次进入未知的远征——带上好奇心，而不是恐惧。

def_en: Specialized skill or knowledge
quote_en: Her expertise in ancient languages made her invaluable to the museum.
quote_zh: 她在古代语言方面的专长使她成为博物馆不可或缺的人才。
ex_en: Expertise without humility becomes arrogance; share what you know, but never stop learning.
ex_zh: 没有谦逊的专长会变成傲慢；分享你所知道的，但永远不要停止学习。

def_en: The act of forcing someone to leave
quote_en: The school threatened expulsion for students caught cheating.
quote_zh: 学校威胁要开除被抓到作弊的学生。
ex_en: The expulsion of a kind thought from your mind is a greater loss than any expulsion from a place.
ex_zh: 将一个善意的想法驱逐出你的头脑，比被驱逐出任何地方都更大的损失。

def_en: Extremely beautiful or delicate
quote_en: The bride wore an exquisite lace gown that took months to make.
quote_zh: 新娘穿着一件花了数月制作的精致蕾丝礼服。
ex_en: An exquisite act of forgiveness can heal a wound that no medicine can reach.
ex_zh: 一次精致的宽恕行为可以治愈任何药物无法触及的伤口。

def_en: Very unusual or remarkable
quote_en: The firefighter showed extraordinary courage in saving the family.
quote_zh: 消防员在拯救那家人时表现出了非凡的勇气。
ex_en: Extraordinary things happen not by magic, but by ordinary people doing ordinary things with extraordinary love.
ex_zh: 非凡的事情不是靠魔法发生的，而是靠普通人以非凡的爱做平凡的事情。

def_en: Excessive spending or luxury
quote_en: Buying a designer bag every month is an extravagance most people cannot afford.
quote_zh: 每月买一个名牌包是大多数人负担不起的奢侈。
ex_en: The greatest extravagance is not money spent, but time wasted on what does not matter.
ex_zh: 最大的奢侈不是花掉的钱，而是浪费在无关紧要之事上的时间。

def_en: An outgoing, socially confident person
quote_en: As an extrovert, she felt recharged after parties and group activities.
quote_zh: 作为一个外向者，她在聚会和集体活动后感到精力充沛。
ex_en: Whether extrovert or introvert, your gift is not in how you act, but in how you care.
ex_zh: 无论是外向还是内向，你的天赋不在于你如何表现，而在于你如何关心。

def_en: A short story with a moral lesson
quote_en: Aesop's fable of the tortoise and the hare teaches that slow and steady wins.
quote_zh: 伊索寓言《龟兔赛跑》教导我们慢而稳者胜。
ex_en: Your life is a fable you write each day; let its moral be kindness.
ex_zh: 你的人生是你每天书写的寓言；让它的寓意是善意。

def_en: The front of a building; a false appearance
quote_en: The old building's facade was restored, but the interior was modernized.
quote_zh: 那座旧建筑的正面被修复了，但内部被现代化了。
ex_en: A cheerful facade can hide a storm inside; be gentle — you never know who is weathering a tempest.
ex_zh: 快乐的外表可以隐藏内心的风暴；要温柔——你永远不知道谁正在经受风暴。

def_en: Capable of making mistakes
quote_en: Humans are fallible, which is why we double-check important calculations.
quote_zh: 人是会犯错的，这就是为什么我们要反复检查重要的计算。
ex_en: To be fallible is to be human; to learn from your fallibility is to be wise.
ex_zh: 会犯错就是人；从你的犯错中学习就是智慧。

def_en: A defect or responsibility for a mistake
quote_en: It was not your fault that the game was cancelled because of rain.
quote_zh: 比赛因下雨取消不是你的错。
ex_en: Do not spend your life looking for whose fault it is; spend it finding whose turn it is to fix it.
ex_zh: 不要花一生寻找是谁的错；花一生寻找轮到谁去修复它。

def_en: An achievement requiring great skill or courage
quote_en: Climbing Mount Everest is a remarkable feat of endurance.
quote_zh: 攀登珠穆朗玛峰是一项非凡的耐力壮举。
ex_en: The greatest feat is not conquering a mountain, but conquering your own fear of trying.
ex_zh: 最伟大的壮举不是征服一座山，而是征服你自己对尝试的恐惧。

def_en: Weak; lacking strength
quote_en: The old man gave a feeble smile and waved goodbye.
quote_zh: 老人露出虚弱的微笑挥手告别。
ex_en: A feeble excuse is worse than no excuse; honesty requires no strength — only courage.
ex_zh: 一个无力的借口比没有借口更糟糕；诚实不需要力量——只需要勇气。

def_en: Fierce and violent
quote_en: The ferocious storm uprooted trees and damaged roofs.
quote_zh: 凶猛的风暴连根拔起树木并损坏了屋顶。
ex_en: A ferocious temper may win a battle, but a gentle spirit wins a lifetime of friendship.
ex_zh: 凶猛的脾气可能赢得一场战斗，但温柔的精神赢得一生的友谊。

def_en: To surprise or shock greatly
quote_en: The magician's final trick flabbergasted the entire audience.
quote_zh: 魔术师的最后一个把戏让整个观众大吃一惊。
ex_en: It will flabbergast you how far a small act of kindness can travel — try it and see.
ex_zh: 一个小小的善举能传播多远会让你大吃一惊——试试看。

def_en: Extremely surprised or shocked
quote_en: I was flabbergasted when my shy friend volunteered to give a speech.
quote_zh: 当我害羞的朋友自愿发表演讲时，我目瞪口呆。
ex_en: Be flabbergasted not by magic, but by the ordinary miracles around you — a sunrise, a friend's loyalty, a second chance.
ex_zh: 让你目瞪口呆的不是魔法，而是你周围的平凡奇迹——日出、朋友的忠诚、第二次机会。

def_en: To indicate or warn of a future event
quote_en: The dark clouds foreshadowed the coming storm.
quote_zh: 乌云预示了即将到来的风暴。
ex_en: Small choices foreshadow great outcomes; pay attention to the seeds you plant today.
ex_zh: 微小的选择预示伟大的结果；注意你今天种下的种子。

def_en: Careful planning or consideration for the future
quote_en: With a little forethought, you can avoid many last-minute problems.
quote_zh: 稍加深谋远虑，你可以避免许多最后一刻的问题。
ex_en: Forethought is not worry; it is the quiet work of building a bridge before you need to cross.
ex_zh: 深谋远虑不是担忧；它是在你需要过河之前建造桥梁的安静工作。

def_en: To swear falsely; to renounce
quote_en: He decided to forswear his old habits and start fresh.
quote_zh: 他决定摒弃旧习惯，重新开始。
ex_en: To forswear a promise is to break a thread in the fabric of trust; weave carefully.
ex_zh: 背弃承诺就是扯断信任织物中的一根线；小心编织。

def_en: A pleasant smell
quote_en: The fragrance of fresh flowers filled the room.
quote_zh: 鲜花的芳香充满了房间。
ex_en: A good reputation has a fragrance that outlasts any perfume; it is carried by the wind of kind deeds.
ex_zh: 好的声誉有一种比任何香水都持久的芳香；它由善行的风携带。

def_en: Obtained or done by deception
quote_en: The company was fined for fraudulent advertising claims.
quote_zh: 该公司因欺诈性广告宣传被罚款。
ex_en: A fraudulent smile may fool a crowd, but it cannot fool the one wearing it.
ex_zh: 一个虚假的微笑可能欺骗一群人，但无法欺骗佩戴它的人。

def_en: Extremely cold; unfriendly
quote_en: The frigid wind made us run for shelter.
quote_zh: 寒冷的风让我们跑向避难所。
ex_en: A frigid response to a warm heart can freeze the warmth in both; thaw with kindness.
ex_zh: 对温暖之心的冷淡回应可以冻结双方的温暖；用善意解冻。

def_en: Not serious or sensible; trivial
quote_en: The court dismissed the lawsuit as frivolous and without merit.
quote_zh: 法院以无意义且无根据为由驳回了诉讼。
ex_en: A frivolous use of time is not always wasted; sometimes laughter is the most serious medicine.
ex_zh: 轻浮地使用时间并不总是浪费；有时笑声是最严肃的药物。

def_en: A person's manner of walking
quote_en: The horse's smooth gait made for a comfortable ride.
quote_zh: 马平稳的步态使骑行舒适。
ex_en: Your gait through life is not measured by speed, but by the steadiness of your direction.
ex_zh: 你人生的步态不是由速度衡量的，而是由你方向的稳定性衡量的。

def_en: Extravagantly bright or showy
quote_en: The gaudy costume was covered in sequins and neon colors.
quote_zh: 那件花哨的戏服上布满了亮片和霓虹色。
ex_en: Gaudy displays may attract attention, but quiet integrity attracts trust.
ex_zh: 花哨的展示可能吸引注意力，但安静的正直吸引信任。

def_en: To cover thinly with gold; to make something seem better
quote_en: The artisan gilded the picture frame with real gold leaf.
quote_zh: 工匠用真金箔给相框镀金。
ex_en: To gild a lie does not make it truth; it only makes it a prettier deception.
ex_zh: 粉饰谎言并不能使它成为真相；只会使它成为更漂亮的欺骗。

def_en: Covered with a thin layer of gold; deceptively attractive
quote_en: The gilded mirror looked expensive but was actually cheaply made.
quote_zh: 那面镀金的镜子看起来昂贵，但实际上制作廉价。
ex_en: A gilded cage is still a cage; seek freedom, not decoration.
ex_zh: 镀金的笼子仍然是笼子；寻求自由，而非装饰。

def_en: Vividly descriptive; relating to visual art
quote_en: The novel contains graphic descriptions of battle scenes.
quote_zh: 这部小说包含对战斗场景的生动描述。
ex_en: A graphic reminder of failure can be a powerful teacher — if you let it, not if you fear it.
ex_zh: 对失败的生动提醒可以是一个强大的老师——如果你允许它，而不是如果你害怕它。

def_en: To trouble or annoy repeatedly
quote_en: It is illegal to harass someone because of their race or religion.
quote_zh: 因种族或宗教骚扰他人是违法的。
ex_en: Do not harass yourself with thoughts of what might have been; turn your gaze to what can still be.
ex_zh: 不要用本可能怎样发生的想法折磨自己；把你的目光转向仍然可能的事情。

def_en: A six-sided polygon
quote_en: The honeycomb is made up of thousands of tiny hexagons.
quote_zh: 蜂巢由成千上万个微小的六边形组成。
ex_en: A hexagon is strong because its angles support each other; a community is strong for the same reason.
ex_zh: 六边形很坚固，因为它的角相互支撑；一个社区坚固也是同样的原因。

def_en: Extremely funny
quote_en: The comedian's jokes were so hilarious that we could not stop laughing.
quote_zh: 喜剧演员的笑话如此滑稽，我们笑个不停。
ex_en: A hilarious moment shared with a friend is a deposit in the bank of joy — withdraw freely.
ex_zh: 与朋友分享的一个滑稽时刻是快乐银行的存款——自由提取。

def_en: Lighting; spiritual or intellectual enlightenment
quote_en: The illumination from the candles created a peaceful atmosphere.
quote_zh: 蜡烛的照明营造了宁静的氛围。
ex_en: The greatest illumination is not a bright room, but a mind that suddenly understands.
ex_zh: 最伟大的照明不是明亮的房间，而是突然理解的思想。

def_en: A false idea or misleading appearance
quote_en: The magician created the illusion that the coin had disappeared.
quote_zh: 魔术师制造了硬币消失的幻觉。
ex_en: The illusion of control is more dangerous than the reality of chaos; embrace what you cannot change.
ex_zh: 控制的幻觉比混乱的现实更危险；拥抱你无法改变的东西。

def_en: Not fully developed; behaving like someone younger
quote_en: His immature response to losing the game was to blame his teammates.
quote_zh: 他对输球的不成熟反应是责怪队友。
ex_en: An immature mind blames others; a mature mind asks, "What could I have done differently?"
ex_zh: 不成熟的心智责怪他人；成熟的心智问：“我本可以做什么不同的事？”

def_en: Excessive; beyond reasonable limits
quote_en: Immoderate consumption of sugary drinks can harm your health.
quote_zh: 过度饮用含糖饮料会损害你的健康。
ex_en: Immoderate pride is a wall that blocks out learning; leave a door open for correction.
ex_zh: 过度的骄傲是一堵挡住学习的高墙；为纠正留一扇门。

def_en: Perfect; faultless
def_zh: 无懈可击的；无可挑剔的
quote_en: Her impeccable manners impressed everyone at the dinner party.
quote_zh: 她无可挑剔的举止给晚宴上的每个人留下了深刻印象。
ex_en: An impeccable reputation is not built by never failing, but by always owning your failures.
ex_zh: 无可挑剔的声誉不是通过从不失败建立的，而是通过始终承认你的失败。

def_en: Impossible to pass through or understand
def_zh: 无法穿透的；难以理解的
quote_en: The fortress had impenetrable walls that no army could breach.
quote_zh: 堡垒有无法穿透的城墙，没有军队能攻破。
ex_en: An impenetrable heart may feel safe, but it also feels nothing else. Let a little light in.
ex_zh: 一颗无法穿透的心可能感到安全，但它也感受不到其他任何东西。让一点光进来。

def_en: Rudeness; disrespectful behavior
quote_en: The student was sent to the principal's office for impertinence.
quote_zh: 那名学生因无礼被送到校长办公室。
ex_en: Impertinence is often a mask for insecurity; respond with patience, not anger.
ex_zh: 无礼常常是不安全感的伪装；以耐心而非愤怒回应。

def_en: Unlikely to be true or happen
def_zh: 不大可能的；未必真实的
quote_en: It is improbable that we will finish the project by tomorrow.
quote_zh: 我们明天之前完成项目是不大可能的。
ex_en: Improbable does not mean impossible; history is full of the improbable becoming fact.
ex_zh: 不大可能并不意味着不可能；历史充满了不大可能变成事实的例子。

def_en: Unable to be reached or entered
quote_en: The remote village is inaccessible by car during winter.
quote_zh: 那个偏远的村庄在冬天无法驾车到达。
ex_en: An inaccessible truth is still true; keep seeking, and the path may open.
ex_zh: 一个难以触及的真理仍然是真理；继续寻找，道路可能会打开。

def_en: Unable to be heard
quote_en: His voice was almost inaudible over the loud music.
quote_zh: 在嘈杂的音乐中，他的声音几乎听不见。
ex_en: The most important things are sometimes inaudible — listen with your heart, not just your ears.
ex_zh: 最重要的事情有时是听不见的——用你的心倾听，而不仅仅是耳朵。

def_en: Not logical or clear; unable to express oneself clearly
quote_en: The patient's speech was incoherent after the accident.
quote_zh: 事故后病人的讲话语无伦次。
ex_en: An incoherent argument can still contain a grain of truth; listen past the confusion.
ex_zh: 一个不连贯的论点仍然可能包含一丝真理；倾听超越混乱之处。

def_en: Lacking the ability or skill to do something
quote_en: The incompetent worker was fired after making too many mistakes.
quote_zh: 那位不胜任的工人在犯了太多错误后被解雇。
ex_en: Calling someone incompetent helps no one; teaching them helps everyone.
ex_zh: 称某人为无能对谁都没有帮助；教导他们则帮助每个人。

def_en: Unable to make decisions quickly
def_zh: 犹豫不决的；优柔寡断的
quote_en: She is indecisive about which college to attend.
quote_zh: 她对上哪所大学犹豫不决。
ex_en: An indecisive mind is not a weak mind; it is one that sees too many possibilities. Learn to choose.
ex_zh: 犹豫不决的心智不是软弱的心智；它是一个看到太多可能性的心智。学会选择。

def_en: Absolutely necessary
def_zh: 不可或缺的；必不可少的
quote_en: A good dictionary is indispensable for learning a new language.
quote_zh: 一本好词典对于学习一门新语言是必不可少的。
ex_en: No person is indispensable, but every person is irreplaceable — a subtle but important difference.
ex_zh: 没有人是不可或缺的，但每个人都是不可替代的——一个微妙但重要的区别。

def_en: Lower in rank, quality, or importance
quote_en: This brand is inferior to the more expensive one in durability.
quote_zh: 这个品牌在耐用性上比更贵的那个差。
ex_en: Feeling inferior is a prison you build yourself; the key is gratitude for what you are, not envy of what you are not.
ex_zh: 感到自卑是你自己建造的监狱；钥匙是对你所是的感恩，而非对你所不是的嫉妒。

def_en: To secretly enter or gain access
quote_en: The spy tried to infiltrate the enemy's headquarters.
quote_zh: 间谍试图潜入敌人的总部。
ex_en: Good habits can infiltrate your life like friendly spies — silently, until they have taken over.
ex_zh: 好习惯可以像友好的间谍一样渗透你的生活——悄无声息，直到它们接管。

def_en: A general increase in prices; the act of filling with air
quote_en: Inflation has made groceries much more expensive this year.
quote_zh: 通货膨胀使今年的食品杂货贵了很多。
ex_en: Inflation of the ego is more dangerous than inflation of money; keep your pride in check.
ex_zh: 自我的膨胀比货币的膨胀更危险；控制你的骄傲。

def_en: To make someone extremely angry
quote_en: The unfair decision infuriated the entire team.
quote_zh: 那个不公平的决定激怒了整个团队。
ex_en: To infuriate is easy; to calm is an art. Practice the art.
ex_zh: 激怒很容易；平静是一门艺术。练习这门艺术。

def_en: Extremely angry
quote_en: She was infuriated when someone took credit for her work.
quote_zh: 当有人把她的工作归功于自己时，她大怒。
ex_en: An infuriated moment can undo years of trust; count to ten before you speak.
ex_zh: 一个愤怒的时刻可以毁掉多年的信任；说话之前数到十。

def_en: The quality of being clever and inventive
quote_en: The engineer's ingenuity solved a problem that had stumped others for years.
quote_zh: 工程师的巧思解决了一个困扰他人多年的问题。
ex_en: Ingenuity is not born from comfort, but from constraint — the tighter the box, the more creative the escape.
ex_zh: 巧思不是从舒适中诞生的，而是从约束中诞生的——箱子越紧，逃脱就越有创意。

def_en: Featuring new methods or ideas
quote_en: The company's innovative approach to recycling won an award.
quote_zh: 公司创新的回收方法赢得了奖项。
ex_en: An innovative solution often comes from someone who did not know it was impossible.
ex_zh: 创新的解决方案常常来自不知道它不可能的人。

def_en: Too many to be counted
quote_en: There are innumerable stars in the night sky.
quote_zh: 夜空中有数不清的星星。
ex_en: Innumerable small kindnesses add up to a mountain of good — start with one.
ex_zh: 无数微小的善举累积成一座善行的大山——从一个开始。

def_en: Curious and eager to learn
quote_en: The inquisitive child asked endless questions about how things work.
quote_zh: 那个好奇的孩子问了无数关于事物如何运作的问题。
ex_en: An inquisitive mind is never bored; the world is an endless book with no final page.
ex_zh: 好奇的心智永远不会无聊；世界是一本没有最后一页的无尽的书。

def_en: To write or carve words onto a surface
quote_en: The jeweler inscribed the ring with their wedding date.
quote_zh: 珠宝商在戒指上刻上了他们的结婚日期。
ex_en: Inscribe kindness on your heart, not as a decoration, but as a guide.
ex_zh: 将善意铭刻在你的心上，不是作为装饰，而是作为指南。

def_en: To intervene on behalf of another
quote_en: The teacher interceded on behalf of the student to reduce the punishment.
quote_zh: 老师为学生说情以减轻惩罚。
ex_en: To intercede for someone is to stand in the gap; it is one of the purest forms of love.
ex_zh: 为某人调解就是站在缺口中；这是最纯粹的爱之一。

def_en: To arouse curiosity; a secret plot
quote_en: The mystery novel's plot intrigued me from the first page.
quote_zh: 那本悬疑小说的情节从第一页就激起了我的兴趣。
ex_en: Do not let intrigue become manipulation; curiosity without respect is a weapon.
ex_zh: 不要让兴趣变成操纵；没有尊重的好奇是一种武器。

def_en: A shy, reserved person who recharges by being alone
quote_en: As an introvert, he prefers reading at home to going to parties.
quote_zh: 作为一个内向者，他更喜欢在家阅读而不是去参加聚会。
ex_en: An introvert's silence is not emptiness; it is a deep ocean where thoughts swim undisturbed.
ex_zh: 内向者的沉默不是空虚；它是一个深海洋，思想在其中不受干扰地游动。

def_en: The ability to understand without conscious reasoning
quote_en: Her intuition told her that something was wrong, even though everything seemed fine.
quote_zh: 她的直觉告诉她有些不对劲，尽管一切看起来都很好。
ex_en: Intuition is not magic; it is the whisper of experience too fast for words. Listen to it.
ex_zh: 直觉不是魔法；它是经验太快而无法言说的低语。倾听它。

def_en: A complete list of items in stock
quote_en: The store manager did a weekly inventory of all products.
quote_zh: 商店经理每周对所有产品进行库存盘点。
ex_en: Take inventory of your blessings daily; you will find you are richer than you thought.
ex_zh: 每天盘点你的福气；你会发现你比你想象的更富有。

def_en: To turn upside down or reverse
quote_en: Invert the bottle to let the last drops of ketchup out.
quote_zh: 把瓶子倒过来让最后几滴番茄酱流出来。
ex_en: To invert a problem is sometimes to see its solution; look from the opposite angle.
ex_zh: 颠倒一个问题有时就是看到它的解决方案；从相反的角度看。

def_en: A person who puts money into something to make a profit
quote_en: The investor put money into the startup in hopes of future returns.
quote_zh: 投资者把钱投入初创公司以期未来回报。
ex_en: The best investment you can make is not in stocks, but in the character of the next generation.
ex_zh: 你能做的最好的投资不是在股票上，而是在下一代的品格上。

def_en: The act of calling upon a higher power for help
quote_en: The ceremony began with an invocation for peace.
quote_zh: 仪式以祈求和平开始。
ex_en: The invocation of a better self is the first step toward becoming it.
ex_zh: 对更好自我的祈求是成为它的第一步。

def_en: To call upon for support or inspiration
quote_en: The lawyer invoked the Fifth Amendment on behalf of his client.
quote_zh: 律师代表他的客户援引了第五修正案。
ex_en: Invoke courage when fear knocks; it will answer.
ex_zh: 当恐惧敲门时召唤勇气；它会回应。

def_en: Cannot be undone or changed
quote_en: Climate change may cause irreversible damage to the planet.
quote_zh: 气候变化可能对地球造成不可逆转的损害。
ex_en: A harsh word is not irreversible; an apology can begin the reversal, even if it cannot erase.
ex_zh: 一句伤人的话并非不可逆转；道歉可以开始逆转，即使无法抹去。

def_en: A short pleasure trip
quote_en: We took a weekend jaunt to the beach.
quote_zh: 我们周末去海滩短途旅行。
ex_en: A jaunt with a friend can refresh a tired soul more than a week of rest alone.
ex_zh: 与朋友的短途旅行比一周独自休息更能让疲惫的灵魂焕然一新。

def_en: To tear or cut deeply
quote_en: The broken glass lacerated his hand.
quote_zh: 碎玻璃割伤了他的手。
ex_en: Words can lacerate deeper than any blade; choose them with care.
ex_zh: 言语可以比任何刀刃割得更深；小心选择它们。

def_en: To criticize harshly
quote_en: The critic lambasted the movie for its weak plot.
quote_zh: 评论家因情节薄弱严厉批评了那部电影。
ex_en: To lambaste is easy; to build up is harder. Choose the harder path.
ex_zh: 严厉批评很容易；建立起来更难。选择更难的路。

def_en: A specific place or location
quote_en: The film was shot in a remote mountain locale.
quote_zh: 这部电影是在一个偏远的山区场所拍摄的。
ex_en: Your character is not determined by your locale, but by what you do there.
ex_zh: 你的品格不是由你的地点决定的，而是由你在那里做什么决定的。

def_en: Very high; noble or grand
quote_en: The mountain had a lofty peak covered in snow.
quote_zh: 那座山有一个白雪覆盖的高耸山峰。
ex_en: Lofty goals without daily steps are just daydreams; climb one foothold at a time.
ex_zh: 没有日常步骤的崇高目标只是白日梦；一次一个立足点地攀登。

def_en: To stand or wait around idly
quote_en: The sign said "No loitering" outside the closed store.
quote_zh: 关门的商店外挂着“禁止闲逛”的牌子。
ex_en: Do not loiter at the doorstep of opportunity; step inside before the door closes.
ex_zh: 不要在机会的门槛上徘徊；在门关闭之前走进去。

def_en: Emitting light; bright
quote_en: The luminous dial of the clock glowed in the dark.
quote_zh: 时钟发光的表盘在黑暗中发光。
ex_en: A luminous spirit does not need a spotlight; it glows from within and warms those nearby.
ex_zh: 一个明亮的精神不需要聚光灯；它从内部发光并温暖附近的人。

def_en: To hide or wait in secret
quote_en: Danger might lurk beneath the calm surface of the water.
quote_zh: 危险可能潜伏在平静的水面之下。
ex_en: Do not let fear lurk in the shadows of your mind; shine the light of reason on it.
ex_zh: 不要让恐惧潜伏在你思想的阴影中；用理性的光照亮它。

def_en: Shining with a soft, reflected light
quote_en: The pearl had a lustrous sheen that caught everyone's eye.
quote_zh: 那颗珍珠有一种吸引所有人目光的光泽。
ex_en: A lustrous reputation is earned drop by drop, like dew on a morning leaf.
ex_zh: 有光泽的声誉是一滴一滴赢得的，就像晨叶上的露水。

def_en: To injure someone permanently
quote_en: The explosion maimed several soldiers.
quote_zh: 爆炸使几名士兵致残。
ex_en: A rumor can maim a reputation more permanently than any physical wound.
ex_zh: 谣言比任何身体创伤更能永久地损害一个人的声誉。

def_en: A skillful move or plan
quote_en: The driver maneuvered the truck into a tight parking space.
quote_zh: 司机熟练地将卡车开进一个狭窄的停车位。
ex_en: Life requires constant maneuver; flexibility is more valuable than strength.
ex_zh: 人生需要不断的策略；灵活性比力量更有价值。

def_en: Small and not important; on the edge
quote_en: The improvement in test scores was marginal, only two points.
quote_zh: 考试分数的提高是微小的，只有两分。
ex_en: A marginal effort yields marginal results; give your full measure.
ex_zh: 微小的努力产生微小的结果；全力以赴。

def_en: A large, stately tomb
quote_en: The ancient king was buried in a marble mausoleum.
quote_zh: 古代国王被埋葬在大理石陵墓中。
ex_en: Do not build a mausoleum for your past mistakes; let them decompose and fertilize new growth.
ex_zh: 不要为过去的错误建造陵墓；让它们分解并滋养新的成长。

def_en: Lacking in quantity or quality
quote_en: The refugees survived on a meager diet of rice and water.
quote_zh: 难民依靠米饭和水组成的贫乏饮食生存。
ex_en: A meager apology is worse than none; say what you mean, or say nothing.
ex_zh: 贫乏的道歉比没有更糟糕；说出你的意思，或者什么也不说。

def_en: Of only average quality; not very good
quote_en: The movie was mediocre — not terrible, but not memorable.
quote_zh: 那部电影很平庸——不算糟糕，但也不令人难忘。
ex_en: A mediocre life is not a failure; it is a life that never risked greatness. Risk something.
ex_zh: 平庸的人生不是失败；它是一种从未冒险追求伟大的人生。冒点险。

def_en: A collection of wild animals
quote_en: The royal menagerie included lions, monkeys, and exotic birds.
quote_zh: 皇家的动物园包括狮子、猴子和异国鸟类。
ex_en: Your fears are a menagerie; open the cage, and they may not be as fierce as they seemed.
ex_zh: 你的恐惧是一个动物园；打开笼子，它们可能不像看起来那么凶猛。

def_en: Relating to weather or climate
quote_en: The meteorological data predicted heavy rain for the weekend.
quote_zh: 气象数据预测周末有大雨。
ex_en: Meteorological uncertainty does not excuse a lack of preparation; pack an umbrella of hope.
ex_zh: 气象的不确定性并不能成为缺乏准备的借口；带上一把希望的伞。

def_en: A form of government with a king or queen as head
quote_en: The United Kingdom is a constitutional monarchy.
quote_zh: 英国是君主立宪制国家。
ex_en: In the monarchy of your own life, you are the sovereign — rule with wisdom, not tyranny.
ex_zh: 在你人生的君主国中，你是君主——以智慧而非暴政统治。

def_en: Dull and repetitive
quote_en: The monotonous sound of the clock ticking made me sleepy.
quote_zh: 时钟滴答的单调声音让我昏昏欲睡。
ex_en: A monotonous routine is not a prison unless you forget to look for small joys within it.
ex_zh: 单调的例行公事不是监狱，除非你忘记在其中寻找微小的快乐。

def_en: Sullen and ill-tempered
quote_en: The morose teenager refused to speak to anyone.
quote_zh: 那个阴郁的青少年拒绝和任何人说话。
ex_en: A morose mood can be a signal, not a sentence — something needs attention, not just endurance.
ex_zh: 阴郁的情绪可以是一个信号，而不是判决——有些事情需要关注，而不仅仅是忍受。

def_en: To think over carefully
quote_en: She mulled over the job offer for several days before deciding.
quote_zh: 她在决定之前仔细考虑了那份工作机会几天。
ex_en: Mull over your words before you release them; once spoken, they cannot be recaptured.
ex_zh: 在说出你的话之前仔细考虑；一旦说出，它们就无法收回。

def_en: To damage or injure severely
quote_en: The vandals mutilated the statue by breaking off its arm.
quote_zh: 破坏者打断雕像的手臂，使其残缺。
ex_en: To mutilate the truth is to wound everyone who depends on it; speak with integrity.
ex_zh: 割裂真相就是伤害每一个依赖它的人；以正直说话。

def_en: The process of planning and following a route
quote_en: The ship's navigation system failed during the storm.
quote_zh: 船上的导航系统在暴风雨中失灵了。
ex_en: The navigation of life requires a compass of values and a map of kindness.
ex_zh: 人生的航行需要一个价值观的指南针和一张善意地图。

def_en: Food or other substances needed for growth
quote_en: The soup provided warm nourishment on a cold day.
quote_zh: 汤在寒冷的日子里提供了温暖的营养。
ex_en: Friendship is nourishment for the soul; without it, even the richest life grows thin.
ex_zh: 友谊是灵魂的营养；没有它，即使最富裕的生活也会变得单薄。

def_en: A nine-day period of prayer
quote_en: The family prayed a novena for their sick grandmother.
quote_zh: 家人为生病的祖母祈祷了九日敬礼。
ex_en: A novena of patience — nine days of holding your tongue — can heal more than a year of arguing.
ex_zh: 九天的耐心——九天的沉默——可以治愈比一年争吵更多的东西。

def_en: The study of the ocean
quote_en: Oceanography explores marine life, currents, and the seafloor.
quote_zh: 海洋学探索海洋生物、洋流和海床。
ex_en: Oceanography teaches us that the deepest places still hold mysteries — so does the human heart.
ex_zh: 海洋学告诉我们，最深的地方仍然蕴藏着奥秘——人心也是如此。

def_en: Having eight sides and eight angles
quote_en: The stop sign is octagonal in shape.
quote_zh: 停车标志是八边形的。
ex_en: An octagonal table invites conversation from every side; be someone who listens from every angle.
ex_zh: 八边形的桌子邀请各方对话；做一个从各个角度倾听的人。

def_en: To leave out or exclude
quote_en: Please do not omit your name from the application form.
quote_zh: 请不要在申请表上遗漏你的名字。
ex_en: To omit a kind word when it is due is to leave a gift unopened.
ex_zh: 在该说善意话语时省略，就像留下一份未打开的礼物。

def_en: A choice or alternative
quote_en: We have two options: walk or take the bus.
quote_zh: 我们有两个选择：步行或坐公交车。
ex_en: When every option seems bad, choose the one that causes the least harm and then do good anyway.
ex_zh: 当每个选择看起来都很糟糕时，选择伤害最小的那个，然后无论如何都去做善事。

def_en: Elaborately decorated
quote_en: The ornate ceiling was covered with gold leaf and painted angels.
quote_zh: 装饰华丽的天花板上覆盖着金箔和手绘的天使。
ex_en: An ornate apology is less valuable than a simple, honest one.
ex_zh: 华丽的道歉不如简单诚实的道歉有价值。

def_en: Above the head; in the sky
quote_en: Birds flew overhead, migrating south for the winter.
quote_zh: 鸟儿在头顶飞过，向南迁徙过冬。
ex_en: Keep your goals overhead like a guiding star, but keep your feet on the ground.
ex_zh: 把你的目标像指路星一样放在头顶，但把脚放在地上。

def_en: A scientist who studies fossils
quote_en: The paleontologist discovered dinosaur bones in the desert.
quote_zh: 古生物学家在沙漠中发现了恐龙骨骼。
ex_en: A paleontologist reads stories written in stone; you read stories written in the choices of each day.
ex_zh: 古生物学家阅读写在石头里的故事；你阅读写在每一天选择中的故事。

def_en: A simple story that teaches a moral lesson
quote_en: The Good Samaritan is a famous parable about helping others.
quote_zh: 好撒玛利亚人是一个关于帮助他人的著名寓言。
ex_en: Your life is a parable; what lesson will it teach those who watch you?
ex_zh: 你的人生是一则寓言；它会教那些观看你的人什么教训？

def_en: A distinct section of writing
quote_en: Each paragraph should focus on a single main idea.
quote_zh: 每个段落应集中在一个主要观点上。
ex_en: A single paragraph can change a mind; a single kind act can change a life.
ex_zh: 一个段落可以改变一个想法；一次善举可以改变一个生命。

def_en: A person trained to assist lawyers
quote_en: The paralegal organized the case files for the trial.
quote_zh: 律师助理为庭审整理了案件档案。
ex_en: A paralegal helps justice find its voice; find your own way to be a helper.
ex_zh: 律师助理帮助正义找到它的声音；找到你自己的方式来做一个帮助者。

def_en: Loss of ability to move; a state of powerlessness
quote_en: The accident caused paralysis in his legs.
quote_zh: 事故导致他的双腿瘫痪。
ex_en: Analysis paralysis — overthinking until you cannot act — is a self-made prison. Break out.
ex_zh: 分析瘫痪——过度思考直到无法行动——是一个自制的监狱。打破它。

def_en: A person trained to provide emergency medical care
quote_en: The paramedic arrived within minutes and treated the injured driver.
quote_zh: 急救员几分钟内赶到并治疗了受伤的司机。
ex_en: A paramedic does not ask whose fault it is; they just help. Be that kind of person.
ex_zh: 急救员不问是谁的错；他们只是帮忙。做那样的人。

def_en: Equipment or accessories associated with an activity
quote_en: The table was covered with painting paraphernalia: brushes, paints, and canvases.
quote_zh: 桌子上摆满了绘画用品：刷子、颜料和画布。
ex_en: Do not confuse the paraphernalia of success with success itself; a title is not a character.
ex_zh: 不要把成功的装备与成功本身混淆；头衔不是品格。

def_en: A trained worker who assists professionals
quote_en: The paraprofessional helped the teacher with classroom management.
quote_zh: 辅助专业人员帮助老师进行课堂管理。
ex_en: Every paraprofessional is a pillar; without them, the roof of many services would collapse.
ex_zh: 每一位辅助专业人员都是一根柱子；没有他们，许多服务的屋顶就会倒塌。

def_en: An organism that lives on another and benefits at its expense
quote_en: Ticks are parasites that feed on the blood of animals.
quote_zh: 蜱虫是以动物血液为食的寄生虫。
ex_en: A grudge is a parasite of the soul; it feeds on your peace and gives nothing back.
ex_zh: 怨恨是灵魂的寄生虫；它以你的平静为食，什么也不回报。

def_en: To take part in an activity
quote_en: Everyone is encouraged to participate in the science fair.
quote_zh: 鼓励每个人都参加科学博览会。
ex_en: To participate is to show up; to contribute is to bring your best self. Do both.
ex_zh: 参与就是出现；贡献就是带来你最好的自己。两者都做。

def_en: To enter or pierce through
quote_en: The drill could not penetrate the thick steel plate.
quote_zh: 钻头无法穿透厚厚的钢板。
ex_en: Kindness can penetrate walls that anger builds; be patient, it works slowly but surely.
ex_zh: 善意可以穿透愤怒建造的墙；耐心点，它缓慢但确定地起作用。

def_en: The outer boundary of an area
quote_en: Soldiers guarded the perimeter of the military base.
quote_zh: 士兵守卫着军事基地的边界。
ex_en: The perimeter of your comfort zone is not a wall; it is a line you can step across.
ex_zh: 你舒适区的边界不是一堵墙；它是一条你可以跨过的线。

def_en: Happening at regular intervals
quote_en: The machine requires periodic maintenance every six months.
quote_zh: 机器每六个月需要定期维护。
ex_en: Periodic silence is not emptiness; it is the soil where wisdom grows.
ex_zh: 定期的沉默不是空虚；它是智慧生长的土壤。

def_en: Allowed or permitted
quote_en: It is not permissible to use your phone during the exam.
quote_zh: 考试期间使用手机是不允许的。
ex_en: Just because something is permissible does not mean it is wise; choose with your conscience.
ex_zh: 仅仅因为某事是被允许的，并不意味着它是明智的；用你的良知选择。

def_en: Continuing firmly despite difficulty
quote_en: Her persistent efforts finally paid off when she won the scholarship.
quote_zh: 她坚持不懈的努力终于得到了回报，她赢得了奖学金。
ex_en: A persistent drop of water can carve a canyon; a persistent heart can change a world.
ex_zh: 一滴坚持不懈的水可以雕刻峡谷；一颗坚持不懈的心可以改变世界。

def_en: To represent an abstract quality as a person
quote_en: In the poem, death is personified as a kind old man.
quote_zh: 在诗中，死亡被拟人化为一个慈祥的老人。
ex_en: Personify patience when others are rushing; you will become a sanctuary for the frantic.
ex_zh: 当别人匆忙时成为耐心的化身；你将成为狂乱者的避难所。

def_en: Of little importance; small-minded
quote_en: The argument was over something petty, like who left the cap off the toothpaste.
quote_zh: 争论是关于琐碎的事情，比如谁没盖上牙膏盖。
ex_en: A petty revenge is a fire that burns the avenger more than the target. Let it go.
ex_zh: 琐碎的报复是烧复仇者比目标更多的火。放手吧。

def_en: A ruler of ancient Egypt
quote_en: The pharaoh ordered the construction of a great pyramid.
quote_zh: 法老下令建造一座大金字塔。
ex_en: A pharaoh's power crumbled to dust; only the stones remember. Build with kindness, not stone.
ex_zh: 法老的权力化为尘土；只有石头记得。用善意建造，而不是石头。

def_en: The highest point or peak
quote_en: Winning the championship was the pinnacle of his athletic career.
quote_zh: 赢得冠军是他运动生涯的顶峰。
ex_en: The pinnacle of success is not a place you arrive at, but a view you earn through the climb.
ex_zh: 成功的顶峰不是你到达的地方，而是你通过攀登赢得的美景。

def_en: To arouse curiosity or anger; a feeling of irritation
quote_en: The mysterious package piqued my interest.
quote_zh: 那个神秘的包裹激起了我的兴趣。
ex_en: A moment of pique can undo a year of trust; breathe before you speak.
ex_zh: 一时的愤怒可以毁掉一年的信任；说话之前先呼吸。

def_en: A dull, overused remark
quote_en: "Everything happens for a reason" is a platitude that doesn't always help.
quote_zh: “事出皆有因”是一句并不总是有用的陈词滥调。
ex_en: A platitude is a coin rubbed smooth by too many hands; mint your own fresh words.
ex_zh: 陈词滥调是一枚被太多手磨平的硬币；铸造你自己新鲜的话语。

def_en: Powerful; having great effect
quote_en: The medicine is potent, so take only half a pill.
quote_zh: 这种药很强效，所以只吃半片。
ex_en: A potent word of encouragement can lift a soul higher than any ladder.
ex_zh: 一句强有力的鼓励话语能比任何梯子更能提升一个灵魂。

def_en: Exact and accurate
quote_en: The carpenter made precise measurements before cutting the wood.
quote_zh: 木匠在切割木材之前做了精确的测量。
ex_en: Be precise in your promises; vagueness is a cousin to deception.
ex_zh: 在承诺上要精确；模糊是欺骗的表亲。

def_en: A person who held a position before another
quote_en: My predecessor left detailed notes to help me learn the job.
quote_zh: 我的前任留下了详细的笔记帮助我学习这份工作。
ex_en: Your predecessor's mistakes are not your inheritance; you can always choose a better way.
ex_zh: 你前任的错误不是你的遗产；你总是可以选择更好的方式。

def_en: A preconceived opinion not based on reason
quote_en: Her prejudice against the new student faded once she got to know him.
quote_zh: 她对新生的偏见在了解他之后消失了。
ex_en: Prejudice is a wall built without a foundation; it stands only as long as no one questions it.
ex_zh: 偏见是一堵没有地基的墙；只要没有人质疑它，它就站立着。

def_en: To order a medicine or treatment; to recommend
quote_en: The doctor prescribed antibiotics for the infection.
quote_zh: 医生为感染开了抗生素。
ex_en: Do not prescribe for others what you are unwilling to take yourself.
ex_zh: 不要为别人开出你自己不愿意服用的东西。

def_en: An animal hunted by another; to hunt or exploit
quote_en: The lion stalked its prey through the tall grass.
quote_zh: 狮子在高草中悄悄接近它的猎物。
ex_en: Do not prey on the weakness of others; lift them instead. That is true strength.
ex_zh: 不要利用他人的弱点；相反，要提升他们。那才是真正的力量。

def_en: Sharing in a secret; informed
quote_en: Only a few people were privy to the CEO's retirement plans.
quote_zh: 只有少数人私下知道CEO的退休计划。
ex_en: Being privy to a secret is a trust; guard it as you would a treasure.
ex_zh: 私下得知一个秘密是一种信任；像守护珍宝一样守护它。

def_en: A high degree of skill
quote_en: His proficiency in Spanish allowed him to translate for the group.
quote_zh: 他精通西班牙语，能够为团队翻译。
ex_en: Proficiency without character is a loaded gun in unsteady hands.
ex_zh: 没有品格的精通是握在不稳定手中的上膛枪支。

def_en: A leisurely walk or a public place for walking
quote_en: Couples promenaded along the beach at sunset.
quote_zh: 情侣们在日落时分沿着海滩散步。
ex_en: A daily promenade of reflection can untangle the knots in your mind.
ex_zh: 每日的反思散步可以解开你心中的结。

def_en: To suggest a plan or idea; to ask someone to marry
quote_en: I propose that we meet earlier to finish on time.
quote_zh: 我提议我们早点见面以便准时完成。
ex_en: Propose solutions, not problems; that is how leaders are made.
ex_zh: 提出解决方案，而不是问题；领导者就是这样炼成的。

def_en: Wealthy and successful
quote_en: The prosperous town had new shops and a growing population.
quote_zh: 那个繁荣的小镇有新商店和不断增长的人口。
ex_en: A prosperous life is measured not by what you have, but by what you have given.
ex_zh: 繁荣的人生不是由你拥有什么衡量的，而是由你给予了什么衡量的。

def_en: A humorous play on words
quote_en: The pun "I used to be a baker, but I couldn't make enough dough" made everyone groan.
quote_zh: 双关语“我以前是个面包师，但我赚不到足够的面团”让所有人都发出了嘘声。
ex_en: A good pun is a handshake between two meanings; a great pun makes you think twice.
ex_zh: 一个好的双关语是两个含义之间的握手；一个伟大的双关语让你三思。

def_en: Having a sharp, strong smell or taste; biting in style
quote_en: The pungent odor of onions made my eyes water.
quote_zh: 洋葱刺鼻的气味让我流泪。
ex_en: A pungent truth is better than a sweet lie, though both are hard to swallow.
ex_zh: 辛辣的真相胜过甜蜜的谎言，尽管两者都难以吞咽。

def_en: To follow in order to catch or achieve
quote_en: She decided to pursue a career in medicine.
quote_zh: 她决定追求医学事业。
ex_en: Pursue excellence, not perfection; excellence is achievable, perfection is a mirage.
ex_zh: 追求卓越，而非完美；卓越是可实现的，完美是海市蜃楼。

def_en: To multiply by four; four times as much
quote_en: The company's profits quadrupled in just two years.
quote_zh: 公司的利润在短短两年内翻了两番。
ex_en: If you quadruple your effort without direction, you will just get lost faster.
ex_zh: 如果你在没有方向的情况下将努力翻两番，你只会更快地迷路。

def_en: To harvest a crop; to receive as a result of actions
quote_en: You reap what you sow — kind actions bring kind rewards.
quote_zh: 种瓜得瓜，种豆得豆——善行带来善报。
ex_en: Do not expect to reap where you have not sown; the fields of life are honest.
ex_zh: 不要指望在没有播种的地方收获；人生的田野是诚实的。

def_en: To take back a statement or belief
quote_en: The heretic was forced to recant his teachings under threat of death.
quote_zh: 异端分子在死亡威胁下被迫放弃他的教义。
ex_en: To recant an error is not weakness; it is the courage to prefer truth over pride.
ex_zh: 放弃错误不是软弱；这是将真理置于骄傲之上的勇气。

def_en: To smell strongly and unpleasantly
quote_en: The garbage can reeked of rotten fish.
quote_zh: 垃圾桶散发着烂鱼的臭味。
ex_en: A lie may hide for a while, but it will eventually reek of its own falseness.
ex_zh: 谎言可能会隐藏一段时间，但最终会散发出虚假的臭味。

def_en: Of or like a king or queen; majestic
quote_en: The cat had a regal bearing, as if it owned the house.
quote_zh: 那只猫有一种帝王般的气度，仿佛它拥有这座房子。
ex_en: A regal spirit is not about power over others, but dignity within yourself.
ex_zh: 帝王般的精神不是对他人的权力，而是你内心的尊严。

def_en: To continue to exist; to stay behind
quote_en: Despite the chaos, she remained calm.
quote_zh: 尽管混乱，她仍然保持冷静。
ex_en: When all else fades, what remains is who you chose to become.
ex_zh: 当其他一切都消失时，剩下的是你选择成为的人。

def_en: A payment sent from one person to another
quote_en: The worker sent a monthly remittance to his family back home.
quote_zh: 那名工人每月向家乡的家人汇款。
ex_en: A remittance of kindness costs nothing but pays infinite dividends.
ex_zh: 善意的汇款不花分文，却支付无限的回报。

def_en: A small remaining part or quantity
quote_en: After the sale, only a few remnants of fabric were left.
quote_zh: 促销之后，只剩下几块布头。
ex_en: Even a remnant of hope can outlast a mountain of despair.
ex_zh: 即使是一丝希望的残余也能比绝望的高山更持久。

def_en: A person who deserts a cause or group
quote_en: The renegade soldier joined the enemy forces.
quote_zh: 那名叛变士兵加入了敌军。
ex_en: A renegade from fear is a pioneer of courage.
ex_zh: 恐惧的叛逃者是勇气的先驱。

def_en: The act of feeling remorse and changing one's ways
quote_en: His repentance was genuine; he apologized and never repeated the mistake.
quote_zh: 他的忏悔是真诚的；他道歉了，并且再也没有重复那个错误。
ex_en: Repentance is not a feeling; it is a turning — a change of direction, not just of mind.
ex_zh: 忏悔不是一种感觉；它是一种转向——方向的改变，而不仅仅是思想的改变。

def_en: The beliefs people hold about someone or something
quote_en: Her reputation as an honest person made her trusted by all.
quote_zh: 她作为诚实的人的声誉使她受到所有人的信任。
ex_en: Your reputation is what others think of you; your character is what you know of yourself. Guard both.
ex_zh: 你的声誉是别人对你的看法；你的品格是你对自己的了解。守护两者。

def_en: To need something; to demand as necessary
quote_en: This recipe requires three eggs and a cup of flour.
quote_zh: 这个食谱需要三个鸡蛋和一杯面粉。
ex_en: To require help is not weakness; to refuse it is often pride.
ex_zh: 需要帮助不是软弱；拒绝帮助常常是骄傲。

def_en: Necessary; a required thing
quote_en: A valid passport is requisite for international travel.
quote_zh: 有效的护照是国际旅行的必要条件。
ex_en: The only requisite for kindness is a willing heart; no other qualification needed.
ex_zh: 善意的唯一必要条件是一颗愿意的心；不需要其他资格。

def_en: To cancel or revoke officially
quote_en: The company rescinded the job offer after discovering false information.
quote_zh: 公司在发现虚假信息后撤销了工作机会。
ex_en: You cannot rescind a kind word once spoken; speak carefully.
ex_zh: 一旦说出，你无法撤销一句善意的话；谨慎说话。

def_en: A large natural or artificial lake used as a water supply
quote_en: The city's reservoir supplies drinking water to millions.
quote_zh: 城市的水库为数百万人提供饮用水。
ex_en: Patience is a reservoir; draw from it slowly, and it will never run dry.
ex_zh: 耐心是一个水库；慢慢汲取，它就永远不会干涸。

def_en: The act of opposing or withstanding
quote_en: The army met little resistance from the weakened enemy.
quote_zh: 军队几乎没有遇到虚弱敌人的抵抗。
ex_en: Resistance to change is the anchor that keeps old ships from sailing to new shores.
ex_zh: 对变化的抵抗是阻止旧船驶向新海岸的锚。

def_en: To attack in return for a perceived injury
quote_en: The coach warned his players not to retaliate against dirty plays.
quote_zh: 教练警告他的球员不要对肮脏的动作进行报复。
ex_en: To retaliate is to become what you hate; to forgive is to break the cycle.
ex_zh: 报复就是变成你所恨的；宽恕就是打破循环。

def_en: To pull back or take back
quote_en: The cat retracted its claws when it jumped off the couch.
quote_zh: 猫从沙发上跳下来时收回了爪子。
ex_en: A wise person retracts a hasty word before it lands; a fool watches it strike.
ex_zh: 智者在其落地之前收回草率的话语；愚者看着它击中。

def_en: Able to be turned back or undone
quote_en: The jacket is reversible — blue on one side, red on the other.
quote_zh: 这件夹克是可翻转的——一面蓝色，一面红色。
ex_en: Not every decision is reversible; pause before you cut what cannot be sewn back.
ex_zh: 并非每一个决定都是可逆的；在你剪断无法缝回的东西之前暂停。

def_en: To return to a previous state or habit
quote_en: The land will revert to forest if left untouched.
quote_zh: 如果不加干预，这片土地将恢复为森林。
ex_en: Do not revert to old habits when stressed; stress reveals character, it does not excuse it.
