ex_zh: 压力大时不要回到旧习惯；压力揭示品格，而不是为它开脱。

def_en: To annoy or irritate
quote_en: His constant teasing began to rile his little sister.
quote_zh: 他不断的取笑开始激怒他的小妹妹。
ex_en: Do not let small things rile you; a calm sea never made a skilled sailor.
ex_zh: 不要让小事激怒你；平静的大海从未造就熟练的水手。

def_en: A wise person; showing wisdom
quote_en: The old sage offered advice that saved the kingdom.
quote_zh: 老智者提出了拯救王国的建议。
ex_en: A sage knows that the wisest answer is sometimes "I don't know."
ex_zh: 智者知道，最明智的答案有时是“我不知道”。

def_en: To walk in a slow, relaxed manner
quote_en: We sauntered through the park, enjoying the spring flowers.
quote_zh: 我们漫步穿过公园，欣赏春天的花朵。
ex_en: Saunter through life with purpose; slow does not mean lost.
ex_zh: 带着目的漫步人生；缓慢并不意味着迷失。

def_en: To enjoy something completely, especially by lingering over it
quote_en: He savored every bite of the chocolate cake.
quote_zh: 他品味了巧克力蛋糕的每一口。
ex_en: Savor the small victories; they are the bricks from which great lives are built.
ex_zh: 品味小小的胜利；它们是建造伟大人生的砖块。

def_en: A grant for education; serious academic study
quote_en: She won a scholarship to study biology at university.
quote_zh: 她赢得了在大学学习生物学的奖学金。
ex_en: The greatest scholarship is not a sum of money, but a lifelong love of learning.
ex_zh: 最伟大的奖学金不是一笔钱，而是终身对学习的热爱。

def_en: Relating to school or education
quote_en: His scholastic achievements earned him a place on the honor roll.
quote_zh: 他的学业成绩使他进入了荣誉榜。
ex_en: Scholastic success is a tool, not a trophy; use it to build, not to boast.
ex_zh: 学业成功是工具，不是奖杯；用它来建造，而不是吹嘘。

def_en: To burn slightly; to become dried out by heat
quote_en: The iron was too hot and scorched the shirt.
quote_zh: 熨斗太热了，把衬衫烫焦了。
ex_en: A harsh word can scorch a friendship; let it cool before you speak.
ex_zh: 一句严厉的话可以灼伤友谊；说话前让它冷却。

def_en: To write carelessly or illegibly
quote_en: He scrawled his name on the paper without looking.
quote_zh: 他看都没看就在纸上潦草地签了名。
ex_en: A hasty scrawl can be read; a hasty apology is harder to decipher.
ex_zh: 潦草的字迹可以被阅读；草率的道歉更难解读。

def_en: Close, critical examination
quote_en: The proposal came under scrutiny from the finance committee.
quote_zh: 该提案受到了财务委员会的仔细审查。
ex_en: Put your actions under the scrutiny of your own conscience before the world judges them.
ex_zh: 在世界评判你的行为之前，先让你的良知仔细审视它们。

def_en: To move quickly with small steps
quote_en: Mice scurried across the floor when the lights went out.
quote_zh: 灯灭时，老鼠匆匆跑过地板。
ex_en: Do not scurry through life so fast that you miss the faces that need your smile.
ex_zh: 不要如此匆忙地度过人生，以至于错过了需要你微笑的面孔。

def_en: An instrument that measures earthquakes
quote_en: The seismograph recorded a minor tremor that lasted three seconds.
quote_zh: 地震仪记录了一次持续三秒的轻微震颤。
ex_en: Your conscience is a seismograph for moral earthquakes; pay attention when it shakes.
ex_zh: 你的良知是道德地震的地震仪；当它震动时要注意。

def_en: A small group of students studying a topic in depth
quote_en: The professor led a seminar on Shakespeare's tragedies.
quote_zh: 教授主持了一场关于莎士比亚悲剧的研讨会。
ex_en: Life is a seminar with no graduation; every day is a lesson.
ex_zh: 人生是一场没有毕业典礼的研讨会；每一天都是一堂课。

def_en: A school for training priests or ministers
quote_en: He attended seminary for three years before becoming a pastor.
quote_zh: 他在成为牧师之前上了三年神学院。
ex_en: A seminary of silence can teach more than a library of noise.
ex_zh: 沉默的神学院比嘈杂的图书馆教得更多。

def_en: Having a saw-like edge
quote_en: The serrated knife cut through the bread easily.
quote_zh: 锯齿刀轻松切开了面包。
ex_en: A serrated edge can cut both ways; so can a sharp tongue.
ex_zh: 锯齿状的边缘可以双向切割；尖锐的舌头也是如此。

def_en: A group of six people or things
quote_en: The jazz sextet played a lively set at the festival.
quote_zh: 爵士六重奏在音乐节上演奏了一套活泼的曲目。
ex_en: A sextet of virtues — patience, kindness, humility, honesty, courage, gratitude — can harmonize any life.
ex_zh: 六种美德的六重奏——耐心、善良、谦逊、诚实、勇气、感恩——可以调和任何人生。

def_en: Poorly made or of low quality
quote_en: The shoddy construction caused the wall to crack after one winter.
quote_zh: 粗制滥造导致墙壁在一个冬天后就开裂了。
ex_en: A shoddy excuse is worse than a shoddy product; at least the product can be fixed.
ex_zh: 粗劣的借口比粗劣的产品更糟糕；至少产品可以被修复。

def_en: To shrink and wrinkle due to heat or dryness
quote_en: The leaves shriveled in the summer heat.
quote_zh: 叶子在夏日的炎热中枯萎了。
ex_en: A heart without gratitude will shrivel like a leaf in autumn; water it daily.
ex_zh: 没有感恩的心会像秋天的叶子一样枯萎；每天浇灌它。

def_en: An afternoon nap or rest
quote_en: In Spain, many shops close for a siesta after lunch.
quote_zh: 在西班牙，许多商店在午饭后关门午睡。
ex_en: A short siesta of silence can reset a frazzled mind better than an hour of noise.
ex_zh: 一段短暂的静默午睡比一小时的噪音更能重置疲惫的心灵。

def_en: The ability to see; something worth seeing
quote_en: The sunset over the ocean was a beautiful sight.
quote_zh: 海上的日落是一道美丽的景象。
ex_en: Do not lose sight of what matters while chasing what glitters.
ex_zh: 在追逐闪光之物时，不要看不见真正重要的东西。

def_en: A location or place
quote_en: The construction site was busy with workers and machines.
quote_zh: 建筑工地上满是工人和机器。
ex_en: The site of your greatest failure can become the site of your greatest lesson.
ex_zh: 你最大失败的地点可以成为你最大教训的地点。

def_en: A brief, minor fight
quote_en: The two armies clashed in a small skirmish before the main battle.
quote_zh: 两军在主要战斗之前发生了一次小冲突。
ex_en: A verbal skirmish need not become a war; agree to disagree and move on.
ex_zh: 一场口角小冲突不必演变成战争；同意不同意见，然后继续前进。

def_en: Dishonest or immoral; sordid
quote_en: The sleazy salesman tricked the elderly couple into buying a worthless product.
quote_zh: 那个卑鄙的推销员欺骗老夫妇购买了毫无价值的产品。
ex_en: A sleazy shortcut may seem tempting, but it always leads to a dead end.
ex_zh: 一条卑鄙的捷径可能看起来很诱人，但它总是通往死胡同。

def_en: Careless and sloppy
quote_en: The slipshod repair job left the leak worse than before.
quote_zh: 马虎的修理工作让漏水比以前更严重。
ex_en: A slipshod apology is worse than no apology; it adds insult to injury.
ex_zh: 马虎的道歉比没有道歉更糟糕；它在伤口上撒盐。

def_en: A buffet offering many different dishes; a wide variety
quote_en: The holiday dinner was a smorgasbord of traditional dishes.
quote_zh: 节日晚餐是传统菜肴的自助餐。
ex_en: Life offers a smorgasbord of experiences; taste widely, but chew thoughtfully.
ex_zh: 生活提供了丰富多彩的体验；广泛品尝，但仔细咀嚼。

def_en: A temporary stay
quote_en: During his sojourn in Paris, he learned to speak French.
quote_zh: 在巴黎逗留期间，他学会了说法语。
ex_en: Life on earth is a brief sojourn; leave it better than you found it.
ex_zh: 地球上的生命是一次短暂的逗留；让它比你发现时更好。

def_en: A fourteen-line poem with a fixed rhyme scheme
quote_en: Shakespeare wrote many famous sonnets about love and time.
quote_zh: 莎士比亚写了许多关于爱情和时间的著名十四行诗。
ex_en: Your life is a sonnet; each day is a line — make it rhyme with kindness.
ex_zh: 你的人生是一首十四行诗；每一天是一行——让它与善意押韵。

def_en: Extremely dirty and unpleasant
quote_en: The prisoners lived in squalid conditions with no clean water.
quote_zh: 囚犯们生活在没有干净水的肮脏环境中。
ex_en: A squalid heart can live in a palace; a clean heart can flourish in a hut.
ex_zh: 肮脏的心可以住在宫殿里；干净的心可以在小屋里茁壮成长。

def_en: A group of lines in a poem
quote_en: The first stanza of the poem describes a peaceful morning.
quote_zh: 这首诗的第一节描述了一个宁静的早晨。
ex_en: Each stanza of your life should move the story forward; do not repeat what has already been said.
ex_zh: 你人生的每一节都应该推动故事向前发展；不要重复已经说过的话。

def_en: A strong, unpleasant smell
quote_en: The stench of rotting garbage filled the alley.
quote_zh: 腐烂垃圾的恶臭充满了小巷。
ex_en: The stench of a lie lingers long after the lie is told; truth has no odor.
ex_zh: 谎言被说出后很久，恶臭仍然挥之不去；真相没有气味。

def_en: To make great effort
quote_en: She strives to improve her grades every semester.
quote_zh: 她每学期都努力提高成绩。
ex_en: Strive not for applause, but for the quiet satisfaction of having done your best.
ex_zh: 努力不是为了掌声，而是为了尽了全力后那种安静的满足感。

def_en: To yield to authority; to present for consideration
quote_en: Please submit your homework by Friday.
quote_zh: 请在周五之前提交你的作业。
ex_en: To submit to fear is to lose your freedom; to submit to wisdom is to gain it.
ex_zh: 向恐惧屈服就是失去自由；向智慧屈服就是获得自由。

def_en: To pay for a service or publication; to agree with an idea
quote_en: I subscribe to a monthly magazine about science.
quote_zh: 我订阅了一份关于科学的月刊。
ex_en: Subscribe to the belief that every person deserves respect, and you will never go bankrupt.
ex_zh: 订阅每个人都值得尊重的信念，你就永远不会破产。

def_en: To support financially
quote_en: The government subsidizes public transportation to keep fares low.
quote_zh: 政府补贴公共交通以保持低票价。
ex_en: Do not subsidize your bad habits with your good intentions; invest in change instead.
ex_zh: 不要用你的好意资助你的坏习惯；转而投资于改变。

def_en: Excellent; of the highest quality
quote_en: The chef prepared a superb meal that impressed all the guests.
quote_zh: 厨师准备了一顿令所有客人印象深刻的极好餐食。
ex_en: A superb character is not born in comfort; it is forged in the fire of challenges.
ex_zh: 卓越的品格不是在舒适中诞生的；它是在挑战的火焰中锻造的。

def_en: The use of symbols to represent ideas
quote_en: In the novel, the green light is a symbol for hope.
quote_zh: 在小说中，绿灯是希望的象征。
ex_en: The symbolism of a single candle lighting another without losing its own flame is the essence of teaching.
ex_zh: 一根蜡烛点亮另一根而不失去自己火焰的象征意义，是教学的本质。

def_en: Artificial; made by chemical synthesis
quote_en: The jacket is made of synthetic fabric that repels water.
quote_zh: 这件夹克由防水的人造纤维制成。
ex_en: A synthetic smile may pass in a photo, but only a genuine one heals a broken heart.
ex_zh: 合成的微笑可能在照片中过关，但只有真诚的微笑才能治愈破碎的心。

def_en: Forbidden by social custom
quote_en: In some cultures, it is taboo to eat certain foods.
quote_zh: 在某些文化中，吃某些食物是禁忌。
ex_en: The only taboo that should never be broken is the taboo against cruelty.
ex_zh: 唯一永远不应打破的禁忌是反对残忍的禁忌。

def_en: An uncontrolled outburst of anger
quote_en: The toddler threw a tantrum when he couldn't have a cookie.
quote_zh: 幼儿因为吃不到饼干而发脾气。
ex_en: An adult's tantrum is just a louder version of the same thing; learn to calm yourself.
ex_zh: 成年人的发脾气只是同一件事的更大声版本；学会让自己平静下来。

def_en: To delay or linger
quote_en: Do not tarry too long, or you will miss the bus.
quote_zh: 不要耽搁太久，否则你会错过公交车。
ex_en: Tarry a moment to breathe before you rush into the next task; haste wastes more time than waiting.
ex_zh: 在冲向下一个任务之前逗留片刻呼吸；匆忙比等待浪费更多时间。

def_en: Sharp in taste; a small pie
quote_en: The lemonade was too tart, so she added sugar.
quote_zh: 柠檬水太酸了，所以她加了糖。
ex_en: A tart reply may win a point, but a gentle one wins a friend.
ex_zh: 尖刻的回答可能赢得一点，但温和的回答赢得一个朋友。

def_en: To provoke or mock
quote_en: The bully taunted the smaller boy for being short.
quote_zh: 恶霸嘲弄那个矮小的男孩。
ex_en: A taunt is a stone thrown; you cannot call it back. Let it lie where it falls.
ex_zh: 嘲弄是一块扔出的石头；你无法叫它回来。让它落在它落下的地方。

def_en: Moderate in behavior or climate
quote_en: The region has a temperate climate with mild winters.
quote_zh: 该地区气候温和，冬季温暖。
ex_en: A temperate response to anger is not weakness; it is the strength of a controlled fire.
ex_zh: 对愤怒的温和回应不是软弱；它是可控之火的强度。

def_en: To melt from ice; to become friendlier
quote_en: The sun will thaw the frozen ground by afternoon.
quote_zh: 太阳将在下午融化冰冻的地面。
ex_en: A kind word can thaw the coldest heart faster than the sun melts ice.
ex_zh: 一句善意的话语能比太阳融化冰更快地融化最寒冷的心。

def_en: To form a theory
quote_en: Scientists theorize that the universe began with a big bang.
quote_zh: 科学家推测宇宙始于一次大爆炸。
ex_en: It is easy to theorize about change; it is harder to be the change.
ex_zh: 理论化改变很容易；成为改变更难。

def_en: To grow and develop well
quote_en: Plants thrive in rich soil with plenty of sunlight.
quote_zh: 植物在阳光充足、土壤肥沃的地方茁壮成长。
ex_en: Kindness thrives where it is practiced, not where it is preached.
ex_zh: 善意在实践的地方茁壮成长，而不是在宣讲的地方。

def_en: The physical features of a landscape
quote_en: The topography of the region includes mountains, valleys, and rivers.
quote_zh: 该地区的地形包括山脉、山谷和河流。
ex_en: The topography of your life is shaped by the valleys of struggle and the peaks of joy; both are necessary.
ex_zh: 你人生的地形由挣扎的山谷和喜悦的山峰塑造；两者都是必要的。

def_en: A disastrous event; a serious drama with a sad ending
quote_en: The loss of the space shuttle was a national tragedy.
quote_zh: 航天飞机的失事是一场国家悲剧。
ex_en: A tragedy can break you or make you; the difference is what you do afterward.
ex_zh: 悲剧可以摧毁你或造就你；区别在于你之后做什么。

def_en: A distinguishing quality or characteristic
quote_en: Patience is a trait that serves you well in life.
quote_zh: 耐心是让你在生活中受益良多的特质。
ex_en: The most valuable trait is not intelligence but the willingness to be taught.
ex_zh: 最有价值的特质不是智力，而是愿意被教导。

def_en: A written or printed copy; an official record of grades
quote_en: The university requested her high school transcript.
quote_zh: 大学要求她的高中成绩单。
ex_en: Your actions are the transcript of your values; read it carefully.
ex_zh: 你的行动是你价值观的文字记录；仔细阅读它。

def_en: To change in form or character
quote_en: The caterpillar transformed into a beautiful butterfly.
quote_zh: 毛毛虫变成了一只美丽的蝴蝶。
ex_en: A single act of forgiveness can transform a relationship more than a thousand apologies.
ex_zh: 一次宽恕的行为比一千次道歉更能改变一段关系。

def_en: Dangerous; betraying trust
quote_en: The icy roads made driving treacherous.
quote_zh: 结冰的道路使驾驶变得危险。
ex_en: A treacherous friend is more dangerous than an open enemy; choose your circle carefully.
ex_zh: 背叛的朋友比公开的敌人更危险；谨慎选择你的圈子。

def_en: Of little importance
quote_en: Do not waste time arguing over trivial matters.
quote_zh: 不要浪费时间争论琐碎的事情。
ex_en: A trivial kindness is not trivial to the one who receives it.
ex_zh: 微不足道的善意对接受它的人来说并不微不足道。

def_en: The act of staying away from school without permission
quote_en: The school warned his parents about his frequent truancy.
quote_zh: 学校警告他的父母他经常逃学。
ex_en: Truancy from your responsibilities is a debt that will be collected with interest.
ex_zh: 逃避责任是一笔会连本带利收回的债务。

def_en: Characterized by conflict or disorder
quote_en: The turbulent flight made many passengers nervous.
quote_zh: 动荡的飞行让许多乘客紧张。
ex_en: A turbulent mind can be calmed by a single deep breath; try it now.
ex_zh: 动荡的头脑可以被一次深呼吸平静下来；现在试试。

def_en: The art of arranging type
quote_en: Good typography makes a book easier to read.
quote_zh: 好的排版使书更容易阅读。
ex_en: The typography of your life is the way you arrange your priorities; make the important letters large.
ex_zh: 你人生的排版是你安排优先事项的方式；把重要的字母放大。

def_en: Leaving no doubt; unambiguous
quote_en: The evidence was unequivocal: he was guilty.
quote_zh: 证据是明确的：他有罪。
ex_en: An unequivocal "no" is kinder than a "maybe" that never comes.
ex_zh: 一个明确的“不”比一个永远不会到来的“也许”更仁慈。

def_en: The state of being identical or consistent
quote_en: The school's dress code promotes uniformity among students.
quote_zh: 学校的着装要求促进了学生之间的统一性。
ex_en: Uniformity may be efficient, but diversity is beautiful; do not confuse sameness with harmony.
ex_zh: 统一性可能是高效的，但多样性是美丽的；不要混淆相同与和谐。

def_en: Not fit to live in
quote_en: After the fire, the house was uninhabitable.
quote_zh: 火灾后，房子不适于居住。
ex_en: An uninhabitable heart is one closed to love; open a window.
ex_zh: 一个不适于居住的心是封闭于爱的心；打开一扇窗。

def_en: Ordinary; not noteworthy
quote_en: The meal was unremarkable, neither good nor bad.
quote_zh: 那顿饭很平凡，不好不坏。
ex_en: An unremarkable life can still be a remarkable gift to those who share it.
ex_zh: 一个平凡的人生仍然可以是对那些分享它的人的非凡礼物。

def_en: To remove from a position or seat
quote_en: The champion was unseated by a younger opponent.
quote_zh: 冠军被一个更年轻的对手击败了。
ex_en: Do not try to unseat someone from a position you are unwilling to fill with integrity.
ex_zh: 不要试图罢免一个你不愿以正直去填补位置的人。

def_en: Importance requiring immediate action
quote_en: The urgency of the situation demanded a quick decision.
quote_zh: 局势的紧迫性要求迅速做出决定。
ex_en: Urgency without direction is panic; direction without urgency is procrastination. Balance them.
ex_zh: 没有方向的紧迫是恐慌；没有紧迫的方向是拖延。平衡它们。

def_en: To make use of
quote_en: We can utilize the old boxes as storage containers.
quote_zh: 我们可以利用旧箱子作为储物容器。
ex_en: Utilize your failures as stepping stones, not as anchors.
ex_zh: 把你的失败当作垫脚石，而不是锚。

def_en: The state of being homeless and wandering
quote_en: The city passed laws to address vagrancy and homelessness.
quote_zh: 该市通过了解决流浪和无家可归问题的法律。
ex_en: Vagrancy of the mind — drifting without purpose — is more common than vagrancy of the body.
ex_zh: 心灵的流浪——没有目的地漂泊——比身体的流浪更常见。

def_en: Absolutely necessary; full of life
def_zh: 至关重要的；充满活力的
quote_en: Water is vital for all living things.
quote_zh: 水对所有生物都是至关重要的。
ex_en: A vital question is not one with a quick answer, but one that changes the asker.
ex_zh: 一个至关重要的问题不是有快速答案的问题，而是改变提问者的问题。

def_en: Open to harm or attack; susceptible
quote_en: The old castle was vulnerable to cannon fire.
quote_zh: 旧城堡容易受到炮火的攻击。
ex_en: To be vulnerable is not weakness; it is the courage to show your cracks so that light can enter.
ex_zh: 脆弱不是软弱；它是展示你的裂缝以便光能进入的勇气。

def_en: To hesitate or be unsteady
quote_en: His confidence never wavered, even under pressure.
quote_zh: 即使在压力下，他的信心也从未动摇。
ex_en: Do not waver between two opinions; choose the one that makes you kinder, then stand by it.
ex_zh: 不要在两种观点之间动摇；选择让你更善良的那一个，然后坚持它。

def_en: A slight smell or trace
quote_en: He caught a whiff of perfume as she walked by.
quote_zh: 当她走过时，他闻到一丝香水味。
ex_en: A whiff of kindness can change the entire atmosphere of a room.
ex_zh: 一丝善意可以改变整个房间的气氛。

def_en: Inspiring wonder; marvelous
quote_en: The child's eyes were full of wondrous delight at the fireworks.
quote_zh: 孩子看到烟花时眼睛里充满了奇妙的喜悦。
ex_en: The most wondrous thing in the universe is not a star, but a person who chooses kindness when it costs them.
ex_zh: 宇宙中最奇妙的东西不是星星，而是一个在需要付出代价时选择善意的人。
